  • [AI summary] “…Inverter 01.02 has been offline since May 27 at 3:29 AM EDT (forced outage, ~750 kWh/day downtime loss on sampl…”
  • [O&M / Theft] 2026-06-24 — CSS / SolRiver O&M — DC wire theft
    DC string wire stolen across the array (~100 runs); of 43 inverters only ~10 online. Fencing also stolen/damaged. SolRiver requested urgent CSS site visit + repair quote and sent theft photos, as-buil
  • [O&M / Theft] 2026-06-24 — SR/NRCO — Elk Inverters Offline / Not Communicating
    Inverters offline from DC-wire theft: 35 of 43 offline by 6/18, ~10 online 6/22, only 3 operational morning of 6/23; 47 rows vandalized. NARENCO techs dispatched 5/28–6/23 restored some strings. Gate
  • [Security] 2026-06-24 — Site security / overnight patrol
    Security procurement after repeated theft. Investigate NC did a 6/11 assessment (drone, perimeter, sheriff reports) and proposed 24/7 guard; SolRiver scoped to overnight only (8pm–6am), short-term, te
  • [Grid / Utility] 2026-06-19 — NARENCO O&M | 06/19/26 Incident Report
    Utility trips restored remotely. WO 50379 Elk: 6/18 19:45 → 6/19 07:15. WO 50380 Sunflower: 6/18 20:30 → 6/19 07:15. WO 50381 Williams: 6/18 20:30 → 6/19 07:15.
→ Confirm utility outage/voltage event for the window; check recloser/breaker closed; verify inverters auto-restarted (production back to expected). Truck roll only if any unit failed to reconnect.
#2Sunflower Solar — Transformer over-temperature fault
  • [alert 06-24 16:47] DIGITAL I/O - XFMR ALARMS (IO1): Transformer Monitor — 90 degree liquid temp fault - Temperature greater than 90 C. 105 degree liquid temp fault (OPEN)
  • [alert 06-24 17:11] DIGITAL I/O - XFMR ALARMS (IO0): Transformer Monitor — 90 degree liquid temp fault - Temperature greater than 90 C. 105 degree liquid temp fault (OPEN)
  • [Grid / Utility] 2026-06-19 — NARENCO O&M | 06/19/26 Incident Report
    Utility trips restored remotely. WO 50379 Elk: 6/18 19:45 → 6/19 07:15. WO 50380 Sunflower: 6/18 20:30 → 6/19 07:15. WO 50381 Williams: 6/18 20:30 → 6/19 07:15.
→ Verify cooling (radiators/fans), oil level and load profile. Sustained >90°C liquid temp degrades insulation — consider derate until inspected.

2026-06-15 Insurance Solar loss at Graham Project
Equipment-loss claim (DOL 2/15/26) involving Solectria inverters. Travelers requested pre-incident O&M reports, alarm logs, work orders, all Solectria communications, warranty/claims docs, and a root cause report. Abhinav replied 6/15 the RCA is not
Full transcript
Hi Tracy,

Thank you for following up. This remains an ongoing situation, and we do
not yet have a finalized root cause analysis report available.

We are actively working on it and will keep you updated as more information
becomes available.

Please let me know if you have any questions in the meantime.

Best regards,
Abhinav

AI Analysis
  • Site producing through evening with light rain; meter shows 22,830 kWh today across METER 2 and METER 3.
  • Several inverters are confirmed offline based on hourly production: INV-4 (since Jun 9 ~1 PM EDT, forced outage — Shutdown Command), INV-7 and INV-9 (since Jun 2, forced outages), INV-13 (since May 21), INV-17 (since Apr 22), INV-22 (Jun 9 ~5 PM, arc fault), INV-23 (since Apr 10), INV-35 (since Apr 6).
  • INV-14 tripped Jun 9 7:21 AM (hardware self-test) but resumed.
  • Combined ~1.4 MW offline; today's loss ~6–8 MWh vs.
  • ~61 MWh daily estimate.

2026-06-22 Regulatory / REC Re: Annual Reconciliation Notice (oversubscription fee)
New OR PUC reconciliation requires oversubscribed participants to repay the utility. SolRiver chose to cover the fees and proposed a single $15,247.38 payment for redistribution, but the PA said it can't redistribute lump-sum funds, so the mechanism
Full transcript
Good afternoon Jess,

We would like to pay the oversubscription fee on behalf of participants.

Thanks,
Steve

On Thu, Jun 4, 2026 at 12:23 PM Jess Siegel <administrator@oregoncsp.org>
wrote:

> Hi all,
>
> The PA recognizes that additional time would be beneficial for Project
> Managers and Subscription Manager to communicate to subscribers about the
> annual bill credit reconciliation process given the recent changes in the
> oversubscription calculation methodology. * In response, the PA will not
> include oversubscription fees in the tomorrow’s (June 5) participant
> billing file and will instead include these fees in the billing file
> processed on Wednesday, July 8. *
>
> The PA is also extending the deadline to June 19, 2026 on our request to
> answer the following questions:
> 1. If you intend to pay the oversubscription fee on behalf of
> participants.
> 2. If you intend to pay oversubscription fees, how you plan to do so.
>
> As a reminder, if you plan to reimburse participants, you may can also
> reach out to the PA to request fee totals that will be included on
> participant bills.
>
> Thank you,
>
> Sincerely,
>
> Jess Siegel
> Program Administrator
>
> Oregon Community Solar Program
> administrator@oregoncsp.org
>
>
>
> On Fri, 29 May at 12:07 PM , Oregon Community Solar Program Administrator
> <administrator@oregoncsp.org> wrote:
>
> Hello,
>
> The Oregon Public Utility Commission approved Staff’s recommended Oregon
> Community Solar Program Implementation Manual (PIM) modifications at the
> May 26, 2026 public meeting with an effective date of May 27, 2026. The
> Staff report can be found here:
> https://apps.puc.state.or.us/orders/2026ords/26-180.pdf.
>
> The approved modifications include a new calculation methodology for the
> annual bill credit reconciliation. Under this update, if the reconciliation
> finds that if the participant’s subscription has generated more kWh than
> the site consumed during the previous twelve months, the Participant wi…
 Steve Maloney / Brandon Conard (SolRiver) ↔ Oregon
AI Analysis
  • Marble Solar is operating normally as of Jun 9 at 5:09 PM PDT.
  • All hardware is communicating, and Inverter 1 produced about 2.6 MW peak earlier today under clear skies and 61°F temperatures.
  • 30-day Energy Performance Index averages 0.99 with inverter availability at 100%.
  • Total production of 758 MWh is 107% of estimate.
  • No major losses identified in the PV model for the sampled inverter; tracker losses are minimal (under 26 kWh/day).

2026-06-29 O&M / Equipment Re: Vegetation Management + PMI — Town of Monroe Solar Farm
Veg management + PMI for Monroe; a tree fell onto modules 6/29 prompting a change order. 		Steve/Brandon/Riley (SolRiver); Danny Avrith/Judy
AI Analysis
  • Inverter 20 has been offline since May 30, confirmed by an Insulation Resistance Low (IsolationErr) fault and stuck-at-zero AC telemetry.
  • Classified as a forced outage affecting ~73 kW (~3.7% of site capacity), with ~2.3 MWh lost over 11 days (~210 kWh/day expected per the PV model losses for the sampled inverter).
  • All other 26 inverters are online and producing in line with peers as of Jun 9 at 8:03 PM EDT.
  • 30-day EPI averages 1.18 and inverter availability is 96%, the latter dragged by INV-20.
  • Sustained EPI above 1.1 and rule tool high-energy-ratio flags across all inverters suggest a PV model or weather station calibration issue.
Monroe Landfill — Ground fault tripping inverter offline
  • [alert 06-24 10:31] Solectria PVI 60KTL Inverter - 22: .Chint / Solectria / Canadian String Inv Faults — Insulation Resistance Low - IsolationErr (4.0h, resolved)
  • [AI summary] “…Inverter 20 has been offline since May 30, confirmed by an Insulation Resistance Low (IsolationErr) fault an…”
→ Do NOT blind-reset. Dispatch electrician: insulation-resistance (megger) test the DC strings, inspect combiner boxes/wiring for water ingress or rodent damage, then clear GFDI and re-energize.

2026-06-29 Regulatory / REC Longleaf & Williams Solar — filing Notice of Termination (NO
Permit closeout (Notice of Termination) for two NC sites. 		Steve/Abhinav (SolRiver) ↔ Chris Bailey (NC DEQ-DE
AI Analysis
  • INVERTER A20 has been offline since Jun 5 with zero production while peers operate normally.
  • The meter-to-inverter ratio of 0.97 confirms a true outage, not a comm loss.
  • A grid overvoltage fault on Jun 8 at 3:10 PM EDT followed by a device communication alert at 3:39 PM points to a forced outage.
  • The other 39 inverters are online and producing normally under clear skies.
  • INVERTER A20 forced outage (Jun 5–present, ongoing ~5 days, 125 kW / 2.5% of site capacity).
Williams Solar, LLC — Grid disturbance / utility event tripped inverters (not equipment failure)
  • [alert 06-24 06:33] RELAY: SEL Recloser Open Alarm — SEL Relay is open (0.1h, resolved)
  • [AI summary] “…INVERTER A20 has been offline since Jun 5 with zero production while peers operate normally. The meter-to-inv…”
→ Confirm utility outage/voltage event for the window; check recloser/breaker closed; verify inverters auto-restarted (production back to expected). Truck roll only if any unit failed to reconnect.
Williams Solar, LLC — Production meter / CT measurement issue — revenue data unreliable
  • [alert 06-24 06:03] METER - PRODUCTION: Device communication — This error may be the result of one of the following: 1. Wiring problem between the devic (0.0h, resolved)
  • [rule tool] 1 performance check(s) FAILING (weather: Partly Cloudy)
→ Compare meter kW vs sum of inverter kW. Phase reading ~0 on one leg → loose CT or wiring. Fix before settlement period closes; backfill from inverter data if needed.
Williams Solar, LLC — UPS battery end-of-life — datalogger will drop on next outage
  • [alert 06-24 15:28] ALSOENERGY SMART UPS: Phoenix Contact - Quint UPS Alerts (QUINT4 EIP) — Battery Voltage Low (0.1h, resolved)
  • [alert 06-24 01:22] ALSOENERGY SMART UPS: Phoenix Contact - Quint UPS Alerts (QUINT4 EIP) — Battery Voltage Low (0.0h, resolved)
→ Replace UPS battery module. Cheap fix that prevents site-dark events during grid blips.

2026-06-24 O&M / Theft CSS / SolRiver O&M — DC wire theft
DC string wire stolen across the array (~100 runs); of 43 inverters only ~10 online. Fencing also stolen/damaged. SolRiver requested urgent CSS site visit + repair quote and sent theft photos, as-builts, and AlsoEnergy screenshots. CSS developing a d 		Steve Maloney (SolRiver) ↔ Ayaz Kiani (Carolina So
2026-06-24 O&M / Theft SR/NRCO — Elk Inverters Offline / Not Communicating
Inverters offline from DC-wire theft: 35 of 43 offline by 6/18, ~10 online 6/22, only 3 operational morning of 6/23; 47 rows vandalized. NARENCO techs dispatched 5/28–6/23 restored some strings. Gate code provided to techs. 		Steve Maloney / Abhinav Korni (SolRiver) ↔ Jayme O
2026-06-24 Security Site security / overnight patrol
Security procurement after repeated theft. Investigate NC did a 6/11 assessment (drone, perimeter, sheriff reports) and proposed 24/7 guard; SolRiver scoped to overnight only (8pm–6am), short-term, terminable at will. 		Steve Maloney (SolRiver) ↔ George Porter (Investig

2026-06-15 Grid / Utility Elk Solar — Smart Inverter Pilot — Need Volt-VAR Enabled
After Elk returned to normal circuit arrangement and the 2 MW limit was removed, volt-VAR/volt-Watt is not enabled, causing over-voltage from reactive-power under-absorption (site may still be in constant power-factor mode). Duke requests re-enabling 		Steve Maloney (SolRiver) ↔ Jayme Orrock (NARENCO);
2026-06-15 Regulatory / REC M-RETS — Issuance Report (Elk Solar)
M-RETS issued Elk Solar RECs: 415 for the April 2026 vintage and 752 for the May 2026 vintage, into SolRiver Capital, LLC (MM2265). 		M-RETS (systemadmin) → John Brown (SolRiver)
AI Analysis
  • Inverter 01.02 has been offline since May 27 at 3:29 AM EDT (forced outage, ~750 kWh/day downtime loss on sampled inverter).
  • Inverter 01.15 also shows zero production with stuck AC telemetry — concurrent outage.
  • Inverters 01.08–01.14 show reduced availability ~30% with ~575–633 kWh/day downtime loss each.
  • Combined ~9 inverter outage represents ~1.1 MW affected capacity, ~5.6 MWh/day loss, ~78 MWh over 14 days.
  • Today's site peak was ~4.3 MW around noon under light rain; weather-limited.
Elk Solar — Grid disturbance / utility event tripped inverters (not equipment failure)
  • [alert 06-24 09:46] Inverter - 03.14: Sungrow Inverter Fault Alert — Islanding (Grid power outage) (OPEN)
  • [alert 06-24 09:51] Inverter - 01.05: Sungrow Inverter Fault Alert — Islanding (Grid power outage) (OPEN)
  • [alert 06-24 09:51] Inverter - 03.11: Sungrow Inverter Fault Alert — Islanding (Grid power outage) (OPEN)
  • [pattern] 4 inverters faulted within 30 min of each other
  • [AI summary] “…Inverter 01.02 has been offline since May 27 at 3:29 AM EDT (forced outage, ~750 kWh/day downtime loss on sampl…”
→ Confirm utility outage/voltage event for the window; check recloser/breaker closed; verify inverters auto-restarted (production back to expected). Truck roll only if any unit failed to reconnect.

Rio Grande Elementary 0.4 MW 13 inverters Degraded6h comms
AI Analysis
  • All 13 Solectria 28kW inverters online and producing as of Jun 9 at 8:02 PM EDT, with the site in light rain and near sunset.
  • Recent production tracks reduced expected output under cloudy conditions, with no outages observed.
  • 30-day Energy Performance Index averages 0.88 and inverter availability is 100%.
  • Site produced 53.0 MWh against 58.1 MWh estimate (91%) per the production meter.
  • Sampled-inverter PV model losses show DC/AC age (~5% each) and ~10% residual; no major events identified.
Rio Grande Elementary — Grid disturbance / utility event tripped inverters (not equipment failure)
  • [pattern] 11 inverters faulted within 30 min of each other
→ Confirm utility outage/voltage event for the window; check recloser/breaker closed; verify inverters auto-restarted (production back to expected). Truck roll only if any unit failed to reconnect.
Rio Grande Elementary — Production meter / CT measurement issue — revenue data unreliable
  • [alert 06-24 00:49] Elkor Production Meter: Power Meter Check 2.0 — Power phase error A = -0.1 kW, B = 0.5 kW, C = 0.0 kW (0.3h, resolved)
  • [alert 06-24 01:19] Elkor Production Meter: Power Meter Check 2.0 — Power phase error A = -0.1 kW, B = 0.0 kW, C = -0.0 kW (8.9h, resolved)
→ Compare meter kW vs sum of inverter kW. Phase reading ~0 on one leg → loose CT or wiring. Fix before settlement period closes; backfill from inverter data if needed.
Rio Grande Elementary — Datalogger / gateway failure — site reporting dark (production may continue)
  • [pattern] 11 of ~13 devices in comm loss
→ Remote-reboot the datalogger / cell modem. If telemetry doesn't return in ~4h, send tech to power-cycle; check cellular signal and modem data contract (contract expiry causes permanent disconnect).

2026-06-19 Land / County / Tax Re: Bulloch County Assessment Appeals
Conflicting valuations: the April audit pegged each site at ~$6.8M FMV while May notices show ~$2.7M each. Income-method argument (favored by SolRiver) apparently not addressed in the re-assessment. County confirmed the audit wave targeted solar proj
Full transcript
Hello,

We have now received an acknowledgement of appeal from Bulloch County.
Please note:

   - (As you know) the audit results sent in *April* place FMV for each
   site at about $6.8 million for 2025
   - The assessment notices sent in *May* list FMV for 2025 at about $2.7
   million for each site, and for 2026 at about $2.7 million each
   - The appeal acknowledgement dated *June 1* seems to be regarding the
   2026 assessment notices put out by the county in May, not regarding the
   audit.
   - The acknowledgement states that, in order to appeal, a certified
   assessment is required by July 15.

Below is a little summary of FMV values put out by the county. I'm
attaching the May assessment notices from the county and the June
acknowledgement of our intent to appeal.

Do you think everyone at the county is on the same page?

Thank you - enjoy your weekend!

Wendy Garduno
Administrator
720-400-8451

[image: image.png]

On Wed, Jun 3, 2026 at 1:49 PM John MacMaster <
john.macmaster@nelsonmullins.com> wrote:

> Wendy—
>
>
>
> I just spoke with Robert Fisher in the Tax Assessor’s office. Mr. Fisher
> indicated that responses to audit/re-assessment appeals would not go out
> for a couple of weeks at the soonest. He also admitted that this wave of
> audits was targeted at solar projects, among others, which makes me think
> that Solriver is not the sole solar project developer in the county. He
> also informed me that he will be taking the County’s role in the appeal,
> but Dan Crumley will also be involved.
>
>
>
> Jack

 An outage has been reported at 2436 GA-HIGHWAY-88 .

 We anticipate that your power will be restored by 06/13 05:00 PM. We will provide updates as more information becomes available. To learn more about outages and the restoration process click here[2].

AI Analysis
  • All 80 inverters are communicating and operating as of Jun 9 at 8:09 PM EDT, with the production meter, weather station, and trackers reporting normally.
  • Light rain is on site at 79°F, with reduced afternoon production today consistent with overcast conditions.
  • No production events in the last two weeks.
  • Daily output has tracked the site performance estimate closely, with full-capacity hours (~9,996 kW) observed Jun 3–5.
  • 30-day EPI averages 0.97 and inverter availability is 99%.
Sunflower Solar — Transformer over-temperature fault
  • [alert 06-24 16:47] DIGITAL I/O - XFMR ALARMS (IO1): Transformer Monitor — 90 degree liquid temp fault - Temperature greater than 90 C. 105 degree liquid temp fault (OPEN)
  • [alert 06-24 17:11] DIGITAL I/O - XFMR ALARMS (IO0): Transformer Monitor — 90 degree liquid temp fault - Temperature greater than 90 C. 105 degree liquid temp fault (OPEN)
→ Verify cooling (radiators/fans), oil level and load profile. Sustained >90°C liquid temp degrades insulation — consider derate until inspected.

2026-06-24 Regulatory / REC M-RETS — Request to Correct Reporting Period End Dates
Shorthorn shown 1/6–2/2/26 should be 1/6–2/3/26; Washington shown 2/1–3/28/26 should be 2/1–2/28/26. Affects REC reporting accuracy.
Full transcript
Hello MRETS Team,

I'm reaching out regarding our generators Shorthorn Holdings (M15837) and
Washington Solar (M17730). I noticed that the reporting period associated
with the most recent entry for each generator contains an incorrect period
end date, and I would like to request a correction.

*Shorthorn Holdings (M15837):*

   - Reporting period on the most recent entry: 1/6/2026 – 2/2/2026
   - Correct reporting period: 1/6/2026 – 2/3/2026

*Washington Solar (M17730):*

   - Reporting period on the most recent entry: 2/1/2026 – 3/28/2026
   - Correct reporting period: 2/1/2026 – 2/28/2026

Could you please let me know the process for correcting these reporting
periods, or if you need any additional information from my end?

Thank you for your assistance.

Best regards,
Abhinav

[image: image.png]
--

*Abhinav Korni*

Asst. Project Manager – *SolRiver Capital LLC*

*o:* (720) 642-6779 | *www.solrivercapital.com*
<http://www.solrivercapital.com/>

===============================================================
 Abhinav Korni (SolRiver) → M-RETS admin

 NARENCO NCC daily report (via projects@)
AI Analysis
  • All 40 inverters communicating as of Jun 9 at 8:08 PM EDT, with site availability at 100% over the last 30 days.
  • Current conditions are light rain at 77°F, and today's production reflects a cloudy/rainy profile, peaking near 3.7 MW at 1:00 PM versus ~5 MW expected.
  • The INVERTER 6 invalid-data warning that triggered at 8:03 PM is a brief end-of-day comm blip; Last Update is current.
  • The 30-day Energy Performance Index averages ~1.16, consistently above 1.1, suggesting a PV model or POA sensor calibration issue worth review.
  • 30-day production is 1,246 MWh (94% of estimate).

2026-06-22 Regulatory / REC Re: Annual Reconciliation Notice (oversubscription fee)
New OR PUC reconciliation requires oversubscribed participants to repay the utility. SolRiver chose to cover the fees and proposed a single $15,247.38 payment for redistribution, but the PA said it can't redistribute lump-sum funds, so the mechanism
Full transcript
Good afternoon Jess,

We would like to pay the oversubscription fee on behalf of participants.

Thanks,
Steve

On Thu, Jun 4, 2026 at 12:23 PM Jess Siegel <administrator@oregoncsp.org>
wrote:

> Hi all,
>
> The PA recognizes that additional time would be beneficial for Project
> Managers and Subscription Manager to communicate to subscribers about the
> annual bill credit reconciliation process given the recent changes in the
> oversubscription calculation methodology. * In response, the PA will not
> include oversubscription fees in the tomorrow’s (June 5) participant
> billing file and will instead include these fees in the billing file
> processed on Wednesday, July 8. *
>
> The PA is also extending the deadline to June 19, 2026 on our request to
> answer the following questions:
> 1. If you intend to pay the oversubscription fee on behalf of
> participants.
> 2. If you intend to pay oversubscription fees, how you plan to do so.
>
> As a reminder, if you plan to reimburse participants, you may can also
> reach out to the PA to request fee totals that will be included on
> participant bills.
>
> Thank you,
>
> Sincerely,
>
> Jess Siegel
> Program Administrator
>
> Oregon Community Solar Program
> administrator@oregoncsp.org
>
>
>
> On Fri, 29 May at 12:07 PM , Oregon Community Solar Program Administrator
> <administrator@oregoncsp.org> wrote:
>
> Hello,
>
> The Oregon Public Utility Commission approved Staff’s recommended Oregon
> Community Solar Program Implementation Manual (PIM) modifications at the
> May 26, 2026 public meeting with an effective date of May 27, 2026. The
> Staff report can be found here:
> https://apps.puc.state.or.us/orders/2026ords/26-180.pdf.
>
> The approved modifications include a new calculation methodology for the
> annual bill credit reconciliation. Under this update, if the reconciliation
> finds that if the participant’s subscription has generated more kWh than
> the site consumed during the previous twelve months, the Participant wi…
 Steve Maloney / Brandon Conard (SolRiver) ↔ Oregon
2026-06-29 Regulatory / REC Arcadia/PGE — Oregon Shines QRE Agreement (Sheridan, Auburn,
Oregon Shines QRE REC compliance for 3 OR sites. 		SolRiver (Steve, Riley) / Perch (Eric Timlin) / PG

  • All hardware is communicating as of Jun 9 at 5:09 PM PDT, with Inverter 1 producing normally through the afternoon under light rain conditions.
  • Today's production tracks expected output closely, peaking near 2.0 MW.
  • Over the last 30 days, the site is performing well: EPI averages 1.09 and inverter availability is 100%.
  • Total production of 553 MWh is 123% of estimate.
  • The persistently elevated EPI (>1.1 on several days) suggests a possible PV model or pyranometer calibration drift worth reviewing.

2026-06-19 Land / County / Tax Re: Bulloch County Assessment Appeals
Conflicting valuations: the April audit pegged each site at ~$6.8M FMV while May notices show ~$2.7M each. Income-method argument (favored by SolRiver) apparently not addressed in the re-assessment. County confirmed the audit wave targeted solar proj
Full transcript
Hello,

We have now received an acknowledgement of appeal from Bulloch County.
Please note:

   - (As you know) the audit results sent in *April* place FMV for each
   site at about $6.8 million for 2025
   - The assessment notices sent in *May* list FMV for 2025 at about $2.7
   million for each site, and for 2026 at about $2.7 million each
   - The appeal acknowledgement dated *June 1* seems to be regarding the
   2026 assessment notices put out by the county in May, not regarding the
   audit.
   - The acknowledgement states that, in order to appeal, a certified
   assessment is required by July 15.

Below is a little summary of FMV values put out by the county. I'm
attaching the May assessment notices from the county and the June
acknowledgement of our intent to appeal.

Do you think everyone at the county is on the same page?

Thank you - enjoy your weekend!

Wendy Garduno
Administrator
720-400-8451

[image: image.png]

john.macmaster@nelsonmullins.com> wrote:

> Wendy—
>
>
>
> I just spoke with Robert Fisher in the Tax Assessor’s office. Mr. Fisher
> indicated that responses to audit/re-assessment appeals would not go out
> for a couple of weeks at the soonest. He also admitted that this wave of
> audits was targeted at solar projects, among others, which makes me think
> that Solriver is not the sole solar project developer in the county. He
> also informed me that he will be taking the County’s role in the appeal,
> but Dan Crumley will also be involved.
>
>

> Jack

AI Analysis
  • All 28 inverters online and reporting fresh telemetry as of Jun 9 at 8:07 PM EDT, with the site past sunset under clear 82°F skies.
  • Earlier today the site reached ~3.0 MW around noon, consistent with AC capacity, though afternoon production was variable with passing clouds.
  • Over the last 30 days, the site averaged an EPI of 0.98 and 99.9% inverter availability.
  • Sampled-inverter PV model losses show only minor age and soiling losses; no major losses identified.
  • Meter-to-inverter ratio is 0.98, normal.
Bulloch 1A — Underperformance without equipment faults — soiling/shading/baseline suspect
  • [alert 06-24 07:44] site: Rule Tool Alert — Errors: , Performance (3) Communication: All tests passed (OPEN)
  • [AI summary] “…Tracker "Position A1 S 16" stuck at 0Â° and AE Smart UPS default alerts not set â monitoring items only. Tracker losses remain small (~24 kWh…”
→ Cross-check irradiance vs output. Clear-day deficit → schedule module wash / vegetation survey; deficit only vs model → recalibrate expected baseline (stale baselines cause ~40% of these alerts).

  • 30-day Energy Performance Index averages 1.01 and inverter availability is 100%.

2026-06-18 Regulatory / REC Re: Gallipolis Solar River Q1 2026 Financials and Production
Live Oak Bank requested Q1 2026 financials + production for Gallipolis. Wendy explained Jan/Feb production was lowered by heavy snow cover and the team will implement a snow management plan for winter.
Full transcript
Alex,

Attached is the Q1 2026 report for Gallipolis Solar River, LLC, which
includes the financial statements and production report.

I talked with our team about the January/February production levels at
Gallipolis - the site was covered with heavy snow for a portion of this
time, which lowered the production. Going forward, we will implement a
snow management plan for the winter months.

Best regards,

Wendy Garduno
720-400-8451

On Thu, Jun 18, 2026 at 7:34 AM Alex Krone <alex.krone@liveoak.bank> wrote:

> Hi Wendy – Can you please send me the Q1 2026 Financials and Production
> for Gallipolis Solar River?
>
>
>
> Thank you,
>

*WENDY GARDUNO*
Administrator - *SolRiver Capital LLC*
1290 N Broadway, Suite 520
Denver, CO 80203

 Wendy Garduno (SolRiver) → Alex Krone (Live Oak Ba
AI Analysis
  • Solectria Inverter - 1 has been offline since Apr 6 at 10:57 AM EDT, last upload confirms ~64 days down.
  • Meter-to-inverter ratio today is ~1.06 — consistent with 1 of 20 inverters not producing, confirming an outage rather than comm loss.
  • The remaining 19 inverters are producing today, with site output tracking expected energy under light rain (73°F).
  • Inverter 12 shows ~29% residual loss versus peers — a persistent underperformance worth field review.
  • Estimated loss from Inverter - 1: ~12 MWh over 64 days (~5% capacity, ~200 kWh/day per PV model losses for the sampled inverter).
Gallia Academy — Production meter / CT measurement issue — revenue data unreliable
  • [alert 06-24 11:24] Elkor Production Meter (Main): Power Meter Check 2.0 — Current mismatch A = 23.6 Amps, B = 28.8 Amps, C = 27.9 Amps (0.4h, resolved)
  • [rule tool] 1 performance check(s) FAILING (weather: Partly Cloudy)
→ Compare meter kW vs sum of inverter kW. Phase reading ~0 on one leg → loose CT or wiring. Fix before settlement period closes; backfill from inverter data if needed.
Gallia Academy — Underperformance without equipment faults — soiling/shading/baseline suspect
  • [rule tool] 1 performance check(s) FAILING (weather: Partly Cloudy)
  • [AI summary] “…Solectria Inverter - 1 has been offline since Apr 6 at 10:57 AM EDT, last upload confirms ~64 days down. Meter-to-inver…”
→ Cross-check irradiance vs output. Clear-day deficit → schedule module wash / vegetation survey; deficit only vs model → recalibrate expected baseline (stale baselines cause ~40% of these alerts).

2026-06-22 Regulatory / REC Re: Annual Reconciliation Notice (oversubscription fee)
New OR PUC reconciliation requires oversubscribed participants to repay the utility. SolRiver chose to cover the fees and proposed a single $15,247.38 payment for redistribution, but the PA said it can't redistribute lump-sum funds, so the mechanism
Full transcript
Good afternoon Jess,

We would like to pay the oversubscription fee on behalf of participants.

Thanks,
Steve

On Thu, Jun 4, 2026 at 12:23 PM Jess Siegel <administrator@oregoncsp.org>
wrote:

> Hi all,
>
> The PA recognizes that additional time would be beneficial for Project
> Managers and Subscription Manager to communicate to subscribers about the
> annual bill credit reconciliation process given the recent changes in the
> oversubscription calculation methodology. * In response, the PA will not
> include oversubscription fees in the tomorrow’s (June 5) participant
> billing file and will instead include these fees in the billing file
> processed on Wednesday, July 8. *
>
> The PA is also extending the deadline to June 19, 2026 on our request to
> answer the following questions:
> 1. If you intend to pay the oversubscription fee on behalf of
> participants.
> 2. If you intend to pay oversubscription fees, how you plan to do so.
>
> As a reminder, if you plan to reimburse participants, you may can also
> reach out to the PA to request fee totals that will be included on
> participant bills.
>
> Thank you,
>
> Sincerely,
>
> Jess Siegel
> Program Administrator
>
> Oregon Community Solar Program
> administrator@oregoncsp.org
>
>
>
> On Fri, 29 May at 12:07 PM , Oregon Community Solar Program Administrator
> <administrator@oregoncsp.org> wrote:
>
> Hello,
>
> The Oregon Public Utility Commission approved Staff’s recommended Oregon
> Community Solar Program Implementation Manual (PIM) modifications at the
> May 26, 2026 public meeting with an effective date of May 27, 2026. The
> Staff report can be found here:
> https://apps.puc.state.or.us/orders/2026ords/26-180.pdf.
>
> The approved modifications include a new calculation methodology for the
> annual bill credit reconciliation. Under this update, if the reconciliation
> finds that if the participant’s subscription has generated more kWh than
> the site consumed during the previous twelve months, the Participant wi…
 Steve Maloney / Brandon Conard (SolRiver) ↔ Oregon

  • No production events occurred in the reporting period.
  • Variable output on Jun 3, Jun 5, and Jun 8 reflects intermittent cloud cover, not equipment issues.
  • 30-day Energy Performance Index averaged 0.99 with inverter availability at 99%.

AI Analysis
  • All 40 inverters are online and producing as of Jun 9 at 8:10 PM EDT, with site output tracking irradiance on a light-rain day per the production and expected chart.
  • A site-wide outage occurred Jun 3–4, with zero meter and inverter production both days visible on the production meter chart.

  • Estimated loss ~83 MWh (2 days, 100% capacity, ~42 MWh/day expected).
  • Production fully recovered Jun 5.
Longleaf Pine Solar, LLC — Transformer over-temperature fault
  • [alert 06-24 06:29] DIGITAL I/O - XFMR ALARMS: Transformer Monitor — Vacuum fault - Pressure less than -5 psi (8.5h, resolved)
→ Verify cooling (radiators/fans), oil level and load profile. Sustained >90°C liquid temp degrades insulation — consider derate until inspected.

  • Meter-to-inverter ratio is 0.98, normal.
Richmond — Underperformance without equipment faults — soiling/shading/baseline suspect
  • [alert 06-24 14:34] site: Rule Tool Alert — Errors: , Performance (7) Communication: 1 test suite failed out of 5 (2 new messages) (OPEN)
→ Cross-check irradiance vs output. Clear-day deficit → schedule module wash / vegetation survey; deficit only vs model → recalibrate expected baseline (stale baselines cause ~40% of these alerts).

2026-06-24 Regulatory / REC M-RETS — Request to Correct Reporting Period End Dates
Shorthorn shown 1/6–2/2/26 should be 1/6–2/3/26; Washington shown 2/1–3/28/26 should be 2/1–2/28/26. Affects REC reporting accuracy.
Full transcript
Hello MRETS Team,

I'm reaching out regarding our generators Shorthorn Holdings (M15837) and
Washington Solar (M17730). I noticed that the reporting period associated
with the most recent entry for each generator contains an incorrect period
end date, and I would like to request a correction.

*Shorthorn Holdings (M15837):*

   - Reporting period on the most recent entry: 1/6/2026 – 2/2/2026
   - Correct reporting period: 1/6/2026 – 2/3/2026

*Washington Solar (M17730):*

   - Reporting period on the most recent entry: 2/1/2026 – 3/28/2026
   - Correct reporting period: 2/1/2026 – 2/28/2026

Could you please let me know the process for correcting these reporting
periods, or if you need any additional information from my end?

Thank you for your assistance.

Best regards,
Abhinav

[image: image.png]
--

*Abhinav Korni*

Asst. Project Manager – *SolRiver Capital LLC*

*o:* (720) 642-6779 | *www.solrivercapital.com*
<http://www.solrivercapital.com/>

===============================================================
 Abhinav Korni (SolRiver) → M-RETS admin
AI Analysis
  • All 72 inverters communicating as of Jun 9 at 8:08 PM EDT, with production tapering normally into evening under light rain.
  • Conditions are intermittently cloudy, consistent with reduced midday output today.
  • No production events in the reporting window.
  • A brief site dip on Jun 5 (EPI 0.81, availability 88%) recovered the same day.
  • 30-day EPI averages 0.97 with inverter availability at 99%.
Shorthorn — Underperformance without equipment faults — soiling/shading/baseline suspect
  • [alert 06-24 12:29] site: Rule Tool Alert — Errors: , Performance (8) Communication: 1 test suite failed out of 5 (3 new messages) (OPEN)

2026-06-16 Grid / Utility Wallace Solar: Allowed to operate at full capacity
Older PGE thread (Mar 2025 MBTT/transfer-trip test cleared the site to full capacity) revived 6/16; PGE's Bryan Focht is locating the right contact and asking whether SolRiver has remote access to the on-site SEL relay to aid diagnostics.
Full transcript
Good morning. I'm recycling this old email in hopes I can find the best contact for the Wallace Solar site located 6475 Wallace Road NW, Salem, OR. More specifically, wondering if you have remote access to the SEL relay on site to aid with some diagnostics.

[cid:image001.png]
Bryan Focht - PE
Protection Testing Engineer
System Protection * C: 541-760-3018 * P: 503-612-6112
Portland General Electric

From: Alix Lefler <alix.lefler@pgn.com>
Sent: Monday, March 3, 2025 3:22 PM
To: John Brown <projects@solrivercapital.com>; Michael Jackson <M.Jackson@KonistoCo.com>; Skylar Cotter <skylar@solrivercapital.com>; Riley Sullivan <riley@solrivercapital.com>
Subject: Wallace Solar: Allowed to operate at full capacity

Good afternoon, team,

We had a successful MBTT test at Wallace Solar last week! The site's transfer trip is currently online, and the site may now operate at full capacity.

As mentioned in our meeting, PGE will take the site temporarily offline on Friday March 7th to test at another site. I will email the Developer and EPC with offline/online updates on Friday.

Thank you all again for your attention and support of this project.

Please reach out with any questions or concerns.

Thank you,

[cid:image002.png]
Alix Lefler
Pronouns: she/her
Staff Construction Project Manager | 503-781-7670
portlandgeneral.com | Follow us on social @PortlandGeneral
An Oregon kind of energy.

---

---

Full transcript
NCC Daily WO Report - Sol River

Ticket #: 50399
Site: Warbler
Start Date | Time: 06/24/2026 | 06:42
End Date | Time: 06/24/2026 | 08:30
Issue: Utility Tripped the site offline at 06:42 on 06/24/2026. Production restored to the site by the NCC remotely at 08:30 on 06/24/2026.

===============================================================

# Part 3 — New & Updated Since 2026-06-27 (6/28–6/30 cycle)

*Grid/utility trips recurred at the NC sites (Elk, Warbler, Whitetail all tripped 6/28; NCC reclosed Whitetail and Washington remotely). The Elk theft response advanced on insurance (Marsh claim kickoff call set for 6/30). A new Bulloch County, GA property-tax track opened — sourcing a certified appraiser (BBG, Keystone, Bowery, GMASS, Smithmyer) for the §48-5-311(f) arbitration plus peer-operator coordination. Also: Longleaf/Williams NOT permit closeout with NCDEQ, Oregon Shines QRE REC reports filed by Perch, and the Solectria spare-inverter lead time (4–5 mo).*
 projects@ (NARENCO NCC) → Brandon; Jayme/Joseph/Ja
2026-06-29 Grid / Utility SR/NRCO — Site Trips/Outages on 6/28
All three NC sites showed 6/28 outages per AlsoEnergy. 		Abhinav Korni (SolRiver) → NARENCO (Jayme Orrock,
AI Analysis
  • All 32 Sungrow SG125 inverters are online and communicating as of Jun 9 at 8:06 PM EDT, with all balance-of-plant devices reporting normally.
  • Today's production was limited by cloud cover; recent clear days (Jun 3–5) reached the 4 MW AC cap during midday hours per the production and expected chart.
  • No discrete production events in the last two weeks.
  • Day-to-day variability on May 22–26 and Jun 1–2 aligns with weather, not equipment issues.
  • 30-day Energy Performance Index averaged 0.88 and inverter availability 97%.
Warbler — Underperformance without equipment faults — soiling/shading/baseline suspect
  • [alert 06-24 11:49] site: Rule Tool Alert — Errors: Performance (6) Communication: All tests passed (OPEN)
  • [alert 06-24 11:36] Sungrow SG125 Inverter A14 at 100%: Inverter / irradiance check — Checking production against available sunlight for 'Sungrow SG125 Inverter A14 at 100%' an (1.6h, resolved)
  • [AI summary] “…ay 22â26 and Jun 1â2 aligns with weather, not equipment issues. 30-day Energy Performance Index averaged 0.88 and inverter availability 97%.…”
→ Cross-check irradiance vs output. Clear-day deficit → schedule module wash / vegetation survey; deficit only vs model → recalibrate expected baseline (stale baselines cause ~40% of these alerts).
Warbler — Grid disturbance / utility event tripped inverters (not equipment failure)
  • [alert 06-24 11:00] Sungrow SG125 Inverter B30 at 100%: Sungrow Inverter Fault Alert — Islanding (Grid power outage) (1.6h, resolved)
  • [alert 06-24 11:00] Sungrow SG125 Inverter B31 at 100%: Sungrow Inverter Fault Alert — Islanding (Grid power outage) (1.5h, resolved)
  • [alert 06-24 11:00] Sungrow SG125 Inverter A13 at 100%: Sungrow Inverter Fault Alert — Islanding (Grid power outage) (1.6h, resolved)
  • [AI summary] “…ay 22â26 and Jun 1â2 aligns with weather, not equipment issues. 30-day Energy Performance Index averaged 0.88 and inverter availability 97%.…”
→ Confirm utility outage/voltage event for the window; check recloser/breaker closed; verify inverters auto-restarted (production back to expected). Truck roll only if any unit failed to reconnect.
Warbler — UPS battery end-of-life — datalogger will drop on next outage
  • [alert 06-24 00:18] AE Smart UPS: Phoenix Contact - Quint UPS Alerts — Battery Mode Activated (12.2h, resolved)
→ Replace UPS battery module. Cheap fix that prevents site-dark events during grid blips.

2026-06-19 DAS / Comms SolRiver Capital Danville IN DAS Rework
Sargent to assess installed DAS vs plan sets. Proposal sent 6/16, revised T&M scope 6/19. No work performed yet.
Full transcript
Sol River Team,

                Please see the attached proposal based on the information received. Please reach out with any questions. Thank you for the opportunity!

Disclaimer

The information contained in this communication from the sender is confidential. It is intended solely for use by the recipient and others authorized to receive it. If you are not the recipient, you are hereby notified that any disclosure, copying, distribution or taking action in relation of the contents of this information is strictly prohibited and may be unlawful.

This email has been scanned for viruses and malware, and may have been automatically archived by Mimecast Ltd, an innovator in Software as a Service (SaaS) for business. Providing a safer and more useful place for your human generated data. Specializing in; Security, archiving and compliance. To find out more visit the Mimecast website.

---

---

Ken,

Thank you for the proposal. I appreciate Sargent's willingness to jump on
this, and I can confirm we are aligned on the general SOW and rate
structure you've outlined.

That said, we need to move from framework to execution. To do that, I need
to hear from your renewables team or the technicians who will be working on
this project and confirm their availability. Specifically, I need
confirmation that they understand the site and have reviewed all the
material I've shared so far which includes the DAS plans, the electrical
as-built drawings, and photos. That review is a prerequisite before we can
finalize the scope and begin the work.

I'd like to schedule a brief Q&A call regarding these materials. Does your
team have availability this week?

Thank you,
Steve

On Tue, Jun 16, 2026 at 12:35 PM Kenneth Wilson <kwilson@sargentelectric.com>
wrote:

> Sol River Team,
>
>
>
> Please see the attached proposal based on the information
> received. Please reach out with any questions. Thank you for the
> opportunity!

> *Disclaimer*

> The information contained in this communicat…
 Steve Maloney (SolRiver) ↔ Ken Wilson / Ben Furrer
2026-06-16 DAS / Comms DAS modem / cell-plan BOM
Stem proposed adding a modem/cell plan (with VPN) to the main DAS (WT200) + exterior antenna; flags the Solectria gateway may not fit the AlsoEnergy enclosure. Quote includes ~12 hrs PM + remote re-commissioning of the DAS units. 		Steve Maloney (SolRiver) ↔ Jack Stringer (Stem / A
2026-06-17 O&M / Equipment Transformer spec sheets
SolRiver was missing technical specs for installed transformers. Hendricks Power supplied the spec sheet (standard 2,500 kVA 3-phase distribution transformers) plus nameplate photos for each installed unit. 		Steve Maloney (SolRiver) ↔ Chuck 'Charllie' Joyce
AI Analysis
  • All 80 SunGrow inverters online and reporting as of Jun 9 at 8:06 PM EDT, with the site producing normally through the day under light rain.
  • Current weather is 77°F with light rain; peak hourly output reached ~9.4 MW around midday per the production and expected chart.
  • 30-day EPI averages 0.94 and inverter availability 99%.
  • Production-to-estimate ratio is 91%.
  • Sampled-inverter PV model losses show no major losses; tracker lost energy is 0 kWh over 7 days.

2026-06-29 50. SR/NRCO - Site Trips/Outages on 6/28 Portfolio (1 msg · abhinav@solrivercapital.com) 
Hi NRCO Team,

AlsoEnergy shows that Elk, Warbler, and Whitetail all experienced outages on 6/28 (Sunday) and did not produce that day.

Was there a reason all three sites tripped? Did you receive any information from Duke regarding a grid outage or other event? If not, please investigate.

Additionally, were the sites reclosed remotely, or did you have to go onsite?

Best regards,
Abhinav

[3 images — production charts for the three sites]

--
Abhinav Korni
Asst. Project Manager – SolRiver Capital LLC
o: (720) 642-6779 | www.solrivercapital.com

---
2026-06-29 51. NARENCO O&M | 06/29/26 Incident Report - Sol River Portfolio (1 msg · projects@solrivercapital.com) 
NCC Daily WO Report - Sol River

| Ticket # | Site | Start Date | Time | End Date | Time | Issue |
| 50425 | Whitetail | 06/27/2026 | 19:27 | 06/28/2026 | 15:47 | Utility Tripped the site offline at 19:27 on 06/27/2026. Production restored to the site by the NCC remotely at 15:47 on 06/28/2026 |
| 50429 | Washington | 06/29/2026 | 04:00 | 06/29/2026 | 09:30 | Utility Tripped the site offline at 04:00 on 06/29/2026. Production restored to the site by the NCC remotely at 09:30 on 06/29/2026 |

SR/Marsh - Elk Solar Property Insurance and BI Claim
When: Tuesday Jun 30, 2026 ⋅ 11:15am – 12pm (Mountain Time - Denver)
Join with Google Meet: meet.google.com/nfm-tdxm-boc

2026-06-29 57. SolRiver / REC Solar - question re property tax in Bulloch County, Georgia Portfolio (1 msg · riley@solrivercapital.com) 
Josh / Katie,

Hope you're doing well - reaching out with a bit of a left-field question here.

We noticed some REC Solar projects operating in Bulloch County. Our question for you all is:

Are you in the process of a property tax audit and/or appeal in Bulloch County, GA?

I ask because SolRiver is currently in this process for 2 of our projects there. Our counsel is hoping to sync up with the counsel of other operators in the area.

Best,
Riley

--
RILEY SULLIVAN
Executive Vice President – SolRiver Capital LLC
o: (720) 548-2327 | www.solrivercapital.com

---
2026-06-29 58. SolRiver / Monarch (Cardinal) - question re property tax in Bulloch County, Georgia Portfolio (1 msg · riley@solrivercapital.com) 
Ajay / Gabe,

Hope you're doing well - reaching out with a bit of a left-field question here.

We noticed some Cardinal Renewables projects operating in Bulloch County. Noting the connection between Monarch > Alchemy > Cardinal, our question for you all is:

Are you in the process of a property tax audit and/or appeal in Bulloch County, GA?

I ask because SolRiver is currently in this process for 2 of our projects there. Our counsel is hoping to sync up with the counsel of other operators in the area.

Best,
Riley

--
RILEY SULLIVAN
Executive Vice President – SolRiver Capital LLC
o: (720) 548-2327 | www.solrivercapital.com

---
2026-06-29 59. SolRiver / Aspen - question re property tax in Bulloch County, Georgia Portfolio (1 msg · riley@solrivercapital.com) 
Dan / Jessica,

Hope you both are doing well.

We noticed there are some Aspen projects operating in Bulloch County, GA (that were previously Safari Energy projects).

Are you in the process of a property tax audit and/or appeal in Bulloch County, GA?

I ask because SolRiver is currently in this process for 2 of our projects there. Our counsel is hoping to sync up with the counsel of other operators in the area.

Feel free to forward on to your asset management team (or others) if they are better equipped to answer.

Best,
Riley

--
RILEY SULLIVAN
Executive Vice President – SolRiver Capital LLC
o: (720) 548-2327 | www.solrivercapital.com

---
2026-06-29 60. SolRiver / Bowery - inquiry re appraisal services for Solar in GA Portfolio (1 msg · riley@solrivercapital.com) 
Brandon,

We're looking for an appraiser to prepare a certified appraisal for a Georgia property tax appeal headed to non-binding arbitration under O.C.G.A. § 48-5-311(f).

For context: SolRiver owns and operates two solar facilities in Bulloch County, GA, each assessed as business personal property. We're disputing the county's value (digest years 2023–2025) and relying on an income approach (DCF) as indicative of fair value, as each project has a long-term contract in place to sell its power.

The statute requires the appraisal be certified by a real property appraiser as classified by the Georgia Real Estate Commission and Georgia Real Estate Appraisers Board.

We were curious if you (or another firm you are aware of) could potentially assist here? We don't necessarily need solar-specific experience; we're mainly seeking a group well-versed in the income approach.

Does this sound like a potential fit for you or another firm you know?

Best,
Riley

--
RILEY SULLIVAN
Executive Vice President – SolRiver Capital LLC
o: (720) 548-2327 | www.solrivercapital.com

---
2026-06-29 61. SolRiver / GMASS - inquiry re appraisal services for Solar in GA Portfolio (1 msg · riley@solrivercapital.com) 
GMASS Team,

We're looking for an appraiser to prepare a certified appraisal for a Georgia property tax appeal headed to non-binding arbitration under O.C.G.A. § 48-5-311(f).

For context: SolRiver owns and operates two solar facilities in Bulloch County, GA, each assessed as business personal property. We're disputing the county's value (digest years 2023–2025) and relying on an income approach (DCF) as indicative of fair value, as each project has a long-term contract in place to sell its power.

The statute requires the appraisal be certified by a real property appraiser as classified by the Georgia Real Estate Commission and Georgia Real Estate Appraisers Board.

We were curious if you (or another firm you are aware of) could potentially assist here? We don't necessarily need solar-specific experience; we're mainly seeking a group well-versed in the income approach.

Does this sound like a potential fit for you or another firm you know?

Best,
Riley

--
RILEY SULLIVAN

o: (720) 548-2327 | www.solrivercapital.com

2026-06-29 62. SolRiver / Smithmyer - inquiry re appraisal services for Solar in GA Portfolio (1 msg · riley@solrivercapital.com) 
John,

We're looking for an appraiser to prepare a certified appraisal for a Georgia property tax appeal headed to non-binding arbitration under O.C.G.A. § 48-5-311(f).

For context: SolRiver owns and operates two solar facilities in Bulloch County, GA, each assessed as business personal property. We're disputing the county's value (digest years 2023–2025) and relying on an income approach (DCF) as indicative of fair value, as each project has a long-term contract in place to sell its power.

The statute requires the appraisal be certified by a real property appraiser as classified by the Georgia Real Estate Commission and Georgia Real Estate Appraisers Board.

We were curious if you (or another firm you are aware of) could potentially assist here? We don't necessarily need solar-specific experience; we're mainly seeking a group well-versed in the income approach.

Does this sound like a potential fit for you or another firm you know?

Best,
Riley

--
RILEY SULLIVAN
Executive Vice President – SolRiver Capital LLC
o: (720) 548-2327 | www.solrivercapital.com

---

We're disputing the county's value (digest years 2023–2025) and relying on an income approach (DCF) as indicative of fair value, as each project has a long-term contract in place to sell its power.

The statute requires the appraisal be certified by a real property appraiser as classified by the Georgia Real Estate Commission and Georgia Real Estate Appraisers Board. Can you confirm whether you or someone on your team qualifies? You don't need solar-specific experience; we're mainly seeking a group familiar with the income approach.

Does this sound like a potential fit? If so, how does your availability look for a call tomorrow?

2026-06-26 44. SR/Balado - Fence Repair at Solar Site - Raeford, NC Portfolio (2 msgs · abhinav@solrivercapital.com) 
*(Attachments: Fence Theft Photo1.png, Fence Theft Photo2.png)*

Hi James,

Following up on our call earlier, we discussed the fence at our solar site in Raeford, NC. The fence is currently damaged, with some sections missing and others detached.

We'd like you to go out to the site this weekend to temporarily secure the damaged fence and help make the site more secure until permanent repairs can be completed. I'm attaching photos of the damaged fence for your reference.

The site information is below:

Elk Solar
547 L McLaughlin Road
Raeford, NC 28376

Based on what we've seen, this appears to be a two-person job. Do you have a partner or another worker who could assist you with this?

Please let us know your hourly rate (and your helper's rate, if applicable) so that we can review and approve the work.

Thank you, and I look forward to hearing from you.

Best regards,
Abhinav

2026-06-25 32. Graham Solar - Inverter RMA Status Update Portfolio (3 msgs · steve@solrivercapital.com) 
Solectria Team,

Please use this thread for all inverter-related updates at the Graham site. I have copied the Solential team for visibility.

Before we move forward with purchasing additional inverters, we need a clear understanding of the status of the failed units and the expected timeline for their replacement. Currently, 9 inverters are offline:

Units: 4, 7, 13, 17, 20, 22, 23, 34, and 35

My understanding is that Inverter 4 failed due to a component issue and Inverters 7, 13, 17, 20, 22, 23, 34, and 35 experienced thermal events.

Can you please confirm whether Solectria has received all of these failed units? Additionally, have replacement units been shipped for the eight inverters that experienced thermal events?

Please provide an update on the RMA status for each failed inverter, including expected shipment and delivery dates for all replacement units. This information will help us determine the timing and scope of our upcoming PO decision.

Thank you,
Steve

--
Steve Maloney
Director of Asset Management – SolRiver LLC
o: 720-923-1983 | www.solriver.com

---

Hi Steve,

So for units received here at Solectria I have a total of 6. The three original from last Aug Inverter number 27, 31, and 3. Recently I received inverters 2, 8, and 9. I do not have the other units.

Thanks,
Tom

> [Quoted: Steve's 6/25 original message above]

--
Thanks,
Tom

(Yaskawa America, Inc. confidentiality disclaimer)

---

Tom

Thank you for the update. Do you need to receive the failed units before sending out the replacements? My understanding is that inverters 7, 13, 17, 22, 23, 34 and 35 were submitted for RMA and have been offline for months waiting for replacements. Can the two new inverters you have in stock be sent out now?

Thank you,
Steve

> [Quoted: Tom's 6/25 reply and Steve's original message above]

--
Steve Maloney
Director of Asset Management – SolRiver LLC
o: 720-923-1983 | www.solriver.com

2026-06-25 38. SR/Solar Gladiator - Urgent Request for Inspection and Repair Quote – Solar Site Theft and Damage Portfolio (1 msg · abhinav@solrivercapital.com) 
Hello,

My name is Abhinav, and I work for SolRiver Capital. We own a solar project located in Raeford, North Carolina that has experienced multiple theft incidents. Portions of the perimeter fence have been damaged and stolen, and approximately 100 runs of DC string wire have been stolen from the site.

We are seeking a qualified contractor to inspect the site, assess the extent of the damage, and provide a proposal for the necessary repairs. The scope of work may include fence repair/replacement, replacement of the stolen DC wire, and repair of any associated damage identified during the inspection.

Due to the impact on site operations and ongoing theft activity, we need to address these issues urgently and would appreciate the earliest possible availability for a site visit and quote. Please let us know if your team can support this request and provide an estimated timeline for inspection and repairs.

We can provide site plans, photographs, and additional information as needed.

Thank you for your time, and we look forward to hearing from you.

Best regards,
Abhinav

--
Abhinav Korni
Asst. Project Manager – SolRiver Capital LLC
o: (720) 642-6779 | www.solrivercapital.com

---

2026-06-25 43. SR/HBIS - Fence Repair – Raeford, NC Solar Project Portfolio (1 msg · abhinav@solrivercapital.com) 
Hello,

This is Abhinav from SolRiver Capital. I'm following up on my call earlier regarding the fence at our Elk solar project in Raeford, NC.

As mentioned during the call, the site has experienced multiple theft incidents, and the fence damage is currently estimated as follows:

- Approximately 700 feet of fencing stolen along the back perimeter
- One additional section of approximately 10 feet removed
- One man-sized hole in a separate section of the fence

We are looking to have the fence repaired and wanted to confirm whether this is a project you are interested in and your earliest availability to begin work.

The site is located at:

547 L McLaughlin Road,
Raeford, NC 28376

Please let us know if you are interested in the project. After assessing the damage, we would appreciate receiving a quote for the repair work, along with your earliest available start date. If you require any additional information before visiting the site, please let me know.

Thank you, and I look forward to hearing from you.

Best regards,
Abhinav

--
Abhinav Korni
Asst. Project Manager – SolRiver Capital LLC
o: (720) 642-6779 | www.solrivercapital.com

2026-06-25 48. Re: Elk Solar - Camera Installation Quote Portfolio (2 msgs · steve@solrivercapital.com) 
Zach,

Thank you for the quote - we are reviewing. Can you confirm the scope includes procurement of the four cameras, poles, and all associated installation and setup?

Additionally, what is the estimated timeline to complete the work once approved?

Thanks,
Steve

> [Quoted: Zach Patterson (HEP) 6/1 original — "Please see the attached proposal for the 4-camera installation scope at Elk Solar."]

--
Steve Maloney
Director of Asset Management – SolRiver LLC
o: 720-923-1983 | www.solriver.com

---

Good afternoon, Steve

That is correct; this proposal does include procurement of the four cameras, poles (we will be utilizing Unistrut), all associated hardware and installment thereof. However, given the concerns of the site's security, we would recommend initializing the new cameras with your team, in-house. The process is simple with a Vosker mobile app that walks you through each step of the commissioning process. But if you would like our team to complete the set up for the new cameras, we can certainly accommodate.

And we anticipate this scope to take two days for a crew of two technicians which we should be able to schedule 1-3 weeks out from approval.

Let us know if you have any other questions.
Thank you!

Zach Patterson
Operations Electrical Foreman, US

hep Energy USA Holdings, Inc.
11330 Vanstory Drive, Suite 101
Huntersville, NC 28078
USA
Mobile: +1 704 661 3651
Office: +1 704 897 2287
Email: z.patterson@hep.global
Website: us.hep.global

> [Quoted: Steve's 6/25 reply and Zach's 6/1 original proposal]

---
2026-06-25 56. Re: Solectria Quote attached. (5 spare inverters) Portfolio (2 msgs · steve@solrivercapital.com) 
Jonathan,

We are nearly in position to approve the quote for the five spare inverters. Before we do, could you please recap the expected shipment timeline? I understand that exact delivery dates can't be guaranteed, but we'd appreciate a general framework for planning purposes.

From our call, my understanding was that two units would be available to ship immediately and the remaining three units would be shipped at a rate of approximately one per month. Is that correct?

Any additional information you can provide regarding the expected shipment schedule and estimated delivery to the site would be very helpful as we finalize our purchasing decision.

Thanks,
Steve

On Fri, Jun 12, 2026 at 2:59 PM Jonathan Helmuth <jonathan.helmuth@solectria.com> wrote:
> Hello SolRiver Capital Team,
> Thank you for your time today on the call. I think we covered a good deal of ground and have a plan moving forward.
> Please see the Quote and T&Cs. Please send your PO to Orders@Solectria.com and CC me so I can expedite.
> Wire instructions are attached. We will do our best to ship these units as soon as we are able.
> Best regards,
> Jonathan Helmuth | Director of Sales
> M: 978.399.9372 | E: Jonathan.Helmuth@Solectria.com
> (Yaskawa America, Inc.)

--
Steve Maloney
Director of Asset Management – SolRiver LLC
o: 720-923-1983 | www.solriver.com

---

Hi Steve,

Our official lead time currently is between 4 and 5 months for new orders. I added safety stock of 2 units for your site at all times. Production is set to build those units in roughly 1 month.

As this site is is a bit of a conundrum, once we have your PO we will expedite the units the best we can. My guess is we would ship out the units in batches as I explained on the phone, likely 2 a month until the PO is fulfilled in full, we can release 2 immediately.

Kind regards,

Jonathan Helmuth
Director of Sales
M: 978.399.9372 | E: Jonathan.Helmuth@Solectria.com

# Email Transcripts — Part 3
2026-06-24 Request to Correct Reporting Period End Dates on Previous Entries Portfolio (1 msg · abhinav@solrivercapital.com) 
Hello MRETS Team,

I'm reaching out regarding our generators Shorthorn Holdings (M15837) and
Washington Solar (M17730). I noticed that the reporting period associated
with the most recent entry for each generator contains an incorrect period
end date, and I would like to request a correction.

*Shorthorn Holdings (M15837):*

   - Reporting period on the most recent entry: 1/6/2026 – 2/2/2026
   - Correct reporting period: 1/6/2026 – 2/3/2026

*Washington Solar (M17730):*

   - Reporting period on the most recent entry: 2/1/2026 – 3/28/2026
   - Correct reporting period: 2/1/2026 – 2/28/2026

Could you please let me know the process for correcting these reporting
periods, or if you need any additional information from my end?

Thank you for your assistance.

Best regards,
Abhinav

[image: image.png]
--

*Abhinav Korni*

Asst. Project Manager – *SolRiver Capital LLC*

*o:* (720) 642-6779 | *www.solrivercapital.com*

2026-06-24 Re: Waynesboro/SolRiver: Renewable Energy Credit Transfers Portfolio (1 msg · vhutchinson@waynesboro.k12.va.us) 
If there is no fee associated with it, we are agreeable to transfer those
credits to Sol River.

On Thu, May 14, 2026 at 4:32 PM Matt La Rocca <larocca@solrivercapital.com>
wrote:

> Hi Vonda,
>
> I hope all has been well. We understand the hesitation for opening a
> trader account to avoid any potential fees involved. That said, we aren't
> aware of another way for us to transfer the credits to your organization.
>
> Accordingly, we are planning to retire the RECs in our account by the end
> of this month and will identify doing so on the school's behalf (if there's
> an option to do so).
>
> Alternatively, we would also be open to amending our SSA so that the
> remaining 20% of RECs (existing and future) is transferred to us - which
> may simplify things.
>
> Please let us know your thoughts or if you have any questions.
>
> Best regards,
>
> Matt
>
> On Wed, Mar 18, 2026 at 11:52 AM Vonda Hutchinson <
> vhutchinson@waynesboro.k12.va.us> wrote:
>
>> After working to set up the trader account and reading the terms
>> and conditions, we may not head in this direction.
>> It seems there is a cost to us associated with this and we would have to
>> get a lawyer to look over the terms and conditions.
>> Thoughts?
>>
>>
>> On Mon, Mar 16, 2026 at 3:05 PM Vonda Hutchinson <
>> vhutchinson@waynesboro.k12.va.us> wrote:
>>
>>> Sorry for our delay. We are going to set up the trader account.
>>> I will start on that in the morning.
>>> Thanks for your patience.
>>>
>>>
>>>
>>> On Mon, Jan 26, 2026 at 1:51 PM Matt La Rocca <
>>> larocca@solrivercapital.com> wrote:
>>>
>>>> Hi Vonda—
>>>>
>>>> No problem.
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> We are currently holding the remaining renewable energy credits (RECs)
>>>> that Waynesboro owns in our PJM-GATS account for the project. There are
>>>> basically 2 options for Waynesboro with these RECs, which are as follows:
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> *Retire the credits* — SolRiver can retire the credits on Waynesboro's
>>>> behalf. Retired credits may be claimed for environmental attributes by
>>>> Waynesboro (but cannot be sold for monetary value).
>>>>
>>>> *Transfer the credits* — We can transfer them to a Waynesboro *Trader
>>>> Account* in PJM-GATS, providing full flexibility for future trading
>>>> (which could include selling to someone else for a monetary value) or other
>>>> use. However, Waynesboro would need to set up a trading account with
>>>> PJM-GATS in order to do this. I've attached a PDF with setup instructions
>>>> (please be sure to select "Trader Account" during registration).
>>>>
>>>> PJM-GATS homepage: https://www.pjm-eis.com
>>>>
>>>> Let us know which option Waynesboro intends to do.
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> Thanks,
>>>> Matt
>>>>
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> On Fri, Jan 23, 2026 at 6:38 AM Vonda Hutchinson <
>>>> vhutchinson@waynesboro.k12.va.us> wrote:
>>>>
>>>>> Please explain the difference.
>>>>> Thank you.
>>>>>
>>>>> On Thu, Jan 22, 2026 at 6:04 PM Matt La Rocca <
>>>>> larocca@solrivercapital.com> wrote:

NCC Daily WO Report - Sol River

Ticket #: 50399
Site: Warbler
Start Date | Time: 06/24/2026 | 06:42

Issue: Utility Tripped the site offline at 06:42 on 06/24/2026. Production restored to the site by the NCC remotely at 08:30 on 06/24/2026.

# Part 3 — New & Updated Since 2026-06-27 (6/28–6/30 cycle)

*Grid/utility trips recurred at the NC sites (Elk, Warbler, Whitetail all tripped 6/28; NCC reclosed Whitetail and Washington remotely). The Elk theft response advanced on insurance (Marsh claim kickoff call set for 6/30). A new Bulloch County, GA property-tax track opened — sourcing a certified appraiser (BBG, Keystone, Bowery, GMASS, Smithmyer) for the §48-5-311(f) arbitration plus peer-operator coordination. Also: Longleaf/Williams NOT permit closeout with NCDEQ, Oregon Shines QRE REC reports filed by Perch, and the Solectria spare-inverter lead time (4–5 mo).*
2026-06-24 54. SR/Marsh - Property Insurance and BI Claims - Elk Solar Portfolio (7 msgs · abhinav@solrivercapital.com) 
Hi Dawn,

Hope you're doing well.

Our solar site, Elk Solar located at 547 L McLaughlin Rd, Raeford, NC 28376, has experienced multiple theft incidents this year, resulting in significant damage to the facility. Thieves damaged and stole portions of the perimeter fence and took substantial amounts of DC wiring from the site.

We would like to initiate both a property insurance claim and a business interruption (BI) claim related to these incidents. I have attached the police reports and some photos documenting the damage and theft activity for your review.

Please let us know if you have any questions or require any additional information. We would also like to schedule a call to discuss the claims process and next steps.

Thank you for your assistance, and we look forward to hearing from you.

Best regards,
Abhinav

[Attachments: Police Report 3.jpeg; Police Report 4.heic; Police Report 2.pdf; Police Report 2_Update.pdf; Police Report 5.pdf; photo 5.jpg; Photo 1.jpg; photo 4.jpg; photo 3.jpg; Photo 2.jpg; Fence Theft Photo1.png; Fence Theft Photo2.png]

--
Abhinav Korni
Asst. Project Manager – SolRiver Capital LLC
o: (720) 642-6779 | www.solrivercapital.com

---

Hi Abhinav,

Per our discussion, there is a $25K deductible that applies to each occurrence of theft.

Please let me know some days/times that work for your team, and I will send an invite to discuss the losses.

Thank you!

Dawn Cruz, CPCU, AMIM
Vice President | Energy and Power Claims Advocate
O: 713 276 2152 | M: 214 543 5864
dawn.cruz@marsh.com
1717 Main Street, 43rd Floor, Dallas, TX 75201

To: Cruz, Dawn <Dawn.Cruz@marsh.com>

[original 6/24 message quoted — Elk Solar theft, perimeter fence + DC wiring, property + BI claim, police reports/photos attached, request to schedule call]

---

Hi Dawn,

Thank you for your response.

We don't have any availability this week, but we are available anytime after 11:00 AM MT on Tuesday, June 30, or Wednesday, July 1. Please let us know what time works best for you.

Looking forward to speaking with you.

Best regards,
Abhinav

2026-06-23 34. Re: Inverter Failure (WCPS William Perry / Kate Collins fuse) Portfolio (5 msgs · steve@solrivercapital.com) 
Ethan,

How many Inverters will be replaced this week? 9 units are currently offline. Have all 9 been sent back to Solectria? Were all 9 approved for RMA? How many replacement units do you currently have? [continues in follow-up messages below]

---

Ethan,

Following up on this, can you please let us know the status of replacing the inverters? We are considering buying a bunch of spare units, but before we do, we need to know what's been done and what's outstanding.

---

Ethan,

Lastly, inverter 4 has been offline since 6/9. Why haven't you replaced the skype [skyper] board component yet? Is this not in the spare parts kit? Inverter 22 was missing from your email. Why is that?

---

Steve,

All inverters get an RMA submitted as soon as they are discovered. After that we just wait to receive them which is normally one at a time from Solectria. At this time, inverter 7, 13, 17, 22, 23, [34, 35 — list of units submitted for RMA].

---

Steve,

A skyper board is a component of the IGBT module which is not in the spare parts kit. It is a very small board but a necessity for the IGBT to function. This was submitted to Solectria for an [RMA/replacement].

---
2026-06-22 35. Re: Request for vegetation management -SolRiver Capital - NY - Town of Monroe Solar Farm LLC Portfolio (10 msgs · davrith@max-solar.com) 
Steve, Was wondering if you received the quote and if you had any questions?

Thank you in advance,
Danny Avrith | MaxSolar
Title: Portfolio Operations Manager
Mobile: (614) 380-6211
450 Raritan Center Parkway, Suite E, Edison, NJ 08837

> [Quoted prior chain: Danny 6/9 PMI quote; Steve 6/8 schedule PMI + mowing ASAP; Danny 6/3 single-cut quote, PMI quote pending; Steve 6/3 questions; Danny 6/3 original vegetation management quote]

---

Good afternoon Danny and Alexa,

Thank you for the quotes. We approve the vegetation management and PMI proposals for the Monroe site.

A few questions and comments:
1. Can you confirm that the PMI scope of work and associated documentation will mirror last year's PMI? We would like to ensure the original contract scope remains in effect for this work.
2. Do you have access to the DAS? We are currently seeing low insulation faults on Inverters 20 and 22, and Inverter 20 has also been offline. Could you please address these issues to the extent possible during the PMI?
3. What is your expected mobilization timeline? It may be beneficial to complete the mowing prior or during the PMI so your techs can more effectively access and inspect the array.

Thank you for your support. I will be in touch in the coming days regarding a long-term service contract.
Best regards,
Steve

> [Quoted: prior chain]

---

Steve, thank you for getting back to us on the two quotes. We are moving forward with scheduling the vegetation management first. Once its done we will follow up on the preventive maintenance (PMI) work order. The scope is the same from previous years and is detailed in the estimate. We have requested our monitoring do a sweep of your site and cases will be created for issues found. Unfortunately, the time allotted for the PM leaves us little room for troubleshooting. Let us see what issues are current at the site as we do see that inverter 20 is offline. Depending on what our team finds we may need to quote this additional work.

Thank you in advance,
Danny Avrith | MaxSolar

> [Quoted: prior chain]

---

Hi Steve,

Please see below for the latest updates regarding your site:
1. Vegetation Management: We have created WO #26024. We will notify you as soon as the schedule is confirmed on our calendar.
2. Preventative Maintenance (PM): WO #26026 has been created and is currently in "New" status. We will proceed with this activity once the vegetation management is complete.
3. Inverter 20 (Zero Generation): This inverter has been offline since May 30th at approximately 12:00 PM EDT. It is currently flagging an "Insulation Error" alert on AE PowerTrack, which typically indicates a short circuit or electrical leakage to the ground on the DC side of the array. Please let us know if you would like a quote to dispatch a technician for troubleshooting.

Thank you.

Kind Regards,
Judy Ann Briones
Jr. POM Specialist
jbriones@max-solar.com
(908) 641-9230 ext. 103

> [Quoted: prior chain]

---

For the vegetation manag…

2026-06-19 Re: Bulloch County Assessment Appeals Bulloch 1ABulloch 1B (1 msg · wendy@solrivercapital.com) 
Hello,

We have now received an acknowledgement of appeal from Bulloch County.
Please note:

   - (As you know) the audit results sent in *April* place FMV for each
   site at about $6.8 million for 2025
   - The assessment notices sent in *May* list FMV for 2025 at about $2.7
   million for each site, and for 2026 at about $2.7 million each
   - The appeal acknowledgement dated *June 1* seems to be regarding the
   2026 assessment notices put out by the county in May, not regarding the
   audit.
   - The acknowledgement states that, in order to appeal, a certified
   assessment is required by July 15.

Below is a little summary of FMV values put out by the county. I'm
attaching the May assessment notices from the county and the June
acknowledgement of our intent to appeal.

Do you think everyone at the county is on the same page?

Thank you - enjoy your weekend!

Wendy Garduno
Administrator
720-400-8451

[image: image.png]

2026-06-18 37. Re: Proposals and Followup (Elk field return — HEP) Portfolio (4 msgs · abhinav@solrivercapital.com) 
Hi Lane,

I work with Steve on Asset Management, and we would like to send HEP back to our Elk site in Raeford if you're available.

There are 43 inverters on-site, and 35 are currently offline. We know some of these are offline due to DC wire theft, but we do not yet know the full extent of the issue.

We'd like HEP to investigate the offline inverters and document all findings with photos to share with us. We would also like an assessment of the missing DC wire and a quote for the materials and labor required to replace it.

As you can imagine, this is a pressing issue. Could you please let us know your earliest availability to visit the site?

Thank you,
Abhinav

> [Quoted prior chain: Steve 5/22 quote follow-up; Steve 4/28 replace ~700 ft fence + signage; Lane 4/28 proposal nearly done, requests linear feet/sign qty; Steve 4/23 agree on signs; Lane 4/22 suggest video surveillance signage deterrent; Steve 4/21 interest inquiry; Steve 4/14 Elk fence stolen again, plan to install 4 Vosker V150 cameras + 2 poles, requests quote; Lane 2/11 follow up on Elk and Shorthorn repairs]

---

Abhinav -

Thanks for reaching out!

And thank you for your patience on this.

I can have a member of our field team go out to the site on Wednesday, if that'll work for you?

Kind regards / Beste Grüße
Lane Ellison
Director of Operations and Maintenance, US
hep Energy USA Delivery, LLC
11330 Vanstory Drive, Suite 101
Huntersville, NC 28078
USA
Phone: +1 704 897 0713
Mobile: +1 704 778 2250

> [Quoted: Abhinav's 6/18 email and full prior chain]

---

Hey Lane,

Jumping in here. We have someone scheduled to be onsite tomorrow to assess the damage related to the stolen DC string wire. Once the assessment is complete, we will share the findings with you to help refine the scope of work and quote.
However, we do know that ~100 runs are missing which might be around 40,000ft. This might help you kick off the quote.

Regarding the fence, approximately 700 feet of 6ft chain link fence has been stolen/damaged and needs replacement. There is also one approximately 10-foot-wide opening in the fence and a second, smaller opening that the thieves cut open that needs repair. Could HEP provide quotes for this?

Thank you,
Steve

> [Quoted: Lane's 6/25 offer, Abhinav's 6/18 email and full prior chain]

---

Hi Lane,

Hope you're doing well.

I'm following up on Steve's email to share the details of the work. Due to DC wire theft, we need to replace 45,000 ft of black source wire and 40,000 ft of red source wire that were stolen.

Could you please provide a quote for this work, along with your availability to perform it and an estimated timeline for completion? Additionally, could you let us know if you have this wire in stock, or if it needs to be ordered?

Thank you, and I look forward to your response.

Best regards,
Abhinav

> [Quoted: Steve's 6/25 email, Lane's 6/25 offer, Abhinav's 6/18 email and full prior chain]

---
