2026-06-17 Re: Annual Reconciliation Notice Portfolio (4 msgs · steve@solrivercapital.com) 
Good afternoon Jess,

We would like to pay the oversubscription fee on behalf of participants.

Thanks,
Steve

On Thu, Jun 4, 2026 at 12:23 PM Jess Siegel <administrator@oregoncsp.org>
wrote:

> Hi all,
>
> The PA recognizes that additional time would be beneficial for Project
> Managers and Subscription Manager to communicate to subscribers about the
> annual bill credit reconciliation process given the recent changes in the
> oversubscription calculation methodology. * In response, the PA will not
> include oversubscription fees in the tomorrow’s (June 5) participant
> billing file and will instead include these fees in the billing file
> processed on Wednesday, July 8. *
>
> The PA is also extending the deadline to June 19, 2026 on our request to
> answer the following questions:
> 1. If you intend to pay the oversubscription fee on behalf of
> participants.
> 2. If you intend to pay oversubscription fees, how you plan to do so.
>
> As a reminder, if you plan to reimburse participants, you may can also
> reach out to the PA to request fee totals that will be included on
> participant bills.
>
> Thank you,
>
> Sincerely,
>
> Jess Siegel
> Program Administrator
>
> Oregon Community Solar Program
> administrator@oregoncsp.org
>
>
>
> On Fri, 29 May at 12:07 PM , Oregon Community Solar Program Administrator
> <administrator@oregoncsp.org> wrote:
>
> Hello,
>
> The Oregon Public Utility Commission approved Staff’s recommended Oregon
> Community Solar Program Implementation Manual (PIM) modifications at the
> May 26, 2026 public meeting with an effective date of May 27, 2026. The
> Staff report can be found here:
> https://apps.puc.state.or.us/orders/2026ords/26-180.pdf.
>
> The approved modifications include a new calculation methodology for the
> annual bill credit reconciliation. Under this update, if the reconciliation
> finds that if the participant’s subscription has generated more kWh than
> the site consumed during the previous twelve months, the Participant will
> need to repay to the Utility the difference between the bill credits
> received for the oversubscribed kWh, and the subscription fees paid on
> those same kWh.
>
> The By Site ID dashboard on the ORCSP platform has been updated to reflect
> the generation and consumption for the entirety of the annual billing
> period (April 1, 2025- March 31, 2026). To identify who is oversubscribed
> in your project, please take the following steps:
>
> - Go to the Participant Consumption page on the ORCSP platform
> - Click on the Site ID tab and download the report
> - Filter for participants who have an “Earliest Billing Start Date” of
> 4/2025 or earlier.
> - Calculate who is oversubscribed by subtracting the “Total Group YTD
> Consumption kWh” by the “Total Group YTD Generation Credit kWh.” Any
> positive number is the amount (in kWh) that the participant site is
> oversubscribed.
>
> PMs and SMs are highly encouraged to notify participants identified as
> oversubscribed as soon as possible…
2026-06-16 Fwd: Requested site information Portfolio (7 msgs · steve@solrivercapital.com) 
---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Investigate NC <pi@investigatenc.com>
Date: Mon, Jun 15, 2026 at 5:31 PM
Subject: Re: Requested site information
To: Steve Maloney <steve@solrivercapital.com>

Mr. Maloney,

Thank you for taking the time to speak with me regarding the security
concerns at the Sol River Capital solar facility located at 547 McLaughlin
Road in Raeford.

Attached is Investigate NC's proposal for providing 24/7 dedicated security
guard and patrol services for the property. In addition to our proposed
security program, I conducted a site assessment on June 11, 2026, which
included aerial drone documentation, perimeter inspections, and a review of
Hoke County Sheriff's Office incident reports related to the facility.

The proposal includes:

• A detailed security plan designed specifically for the property's size,
layout, and history of theft-related incidents.
• GPS-verified perimeter patrol procedures.
• Belfry Guard Tour reporting and accountability measures.
• Site assessment findings and security observations.
• Aerial property overview photographs.
• Documentation of perimeter fence damage.
• Observations regarding vegetation and visibility concerns affecting
security operations.
• Summaries of reported criminal incidents occurring at the property.

Based upon my observations and review of the available information, I
believe a dedicated security presence combined with routine perimeter
patrols and documented reporting can significantly reduce opportunities for
future theft, vandalism, and unauthorized access.

I appreciate the opportunity to submit this proposal and would welcome the
chance to discuss any questions, modifications, or additional security
recommendations that may assist Sol River Capital in protecting this asset.

I look forward to speaking with you.

Respectfully,

George

*George Porter*
*Private Investigator*
*Executive Protection*
*Security Guard & Patrol*
*Firearms Instructor*

NCISS, Government Relations Committee/Legislative Advisory Board
NCCCIA, Regional Director

*CONFIDENTIALITY NOTICE*: This email and any attachments are intended
solely for the use of the individual(s) or entity(ies) to whom they are
addressed and may contain confidential, proprietary, privileged,
investigative, security-sensitive, or legally protected information. Any
unauthorized review, use, disclosure, distributi…

2026-06-16 SolRiver Capital Danville IN DAS Rework Whitetail (17 msgs · kwilson@sargentelectric.com) 
Sol River Team,

                Please see the attached proposal based on the information received. Please reach out with any questions. Thank you for the opportunity!

Disclaimer

The information contained in this communication from the sender is confidential. It is intended solely for use by the recipient and others authorized to receive it. If you are not the recipient, you are hereby notified that any disclosure, copying, distribution or taking action in relation of the contents of this information is strictly prohibited and may be unlawful.

This email has been scanned for viruses and malware, and may have been automatically archived by Mimecast Ltd, an innovator in Software as a Service (SaaS) for business. Providing a safer and more useful place for your human generated data. Specializing in; Security, archiving and compliance. To find out more visit the Mimecast website.

---

---

Ken,

Thank you for the proposal. I appreciate Sargent's willingness to jump on
this, and I can confirm we are aligned on the general SOW and rate
structure you've outlined.

That said, we need to move from framework to execution. To do that, I need
to hear from your renewables team or the technicians who will be working on
this project and confirm their availability. Specifically, I need
confirmation that they understand the site and have reviewed all the
material I've shared so far which includes the DAS plans, the electrical
as-built drawings, and photos. That review is a prerequisite before we can
finalize the scope and begin the work.

I'd like to schedule a brief Q&A call regarding these materials. Does your
team have availability this week?

Thank you,
Steve

I hope all is well.

On Fri, Jun 5, 2026 at 5:28 PM Brandon Conard <brandon@solrivercapital.com>
wrote:

> Thank you for sending all this. Our team is digging in.
>
>
> BRANDON CONARD
> Managing Partner – SolRiver Capital LLC
> o: 720-593-1158 | www.solrivercapital.com
>
> On Fri, Jun 5, 2026, 6:04 AM Joe Parsons <jparsons@hendrickspower.com>
> wrote:
>
>> Brandon-
>>
>>
>>
>> Here is an exploded view of what the RMS voltage is doing when the
>> flicker is triggered on our analyzer. CH4 is floating so you don’t need to
>> pay attention to it, I only included it to get the timescale. Note that
>> the interval is set to 1 second. This graph takes the MAX, MIN, and AVG
>> rms voltage and current at each interval. As discussed, the average
>> voltage is within the nominal range and would not trigger any events or
>> protection on our end. Look at the portions of the graph and take note of
>> the difference between the MAX (red) and the MIN (dark green) and compare
>> it to the time interval at the bottom. What this tells me is that for a
>> duration of minutes at a time you have rapid fluctuations in voltage which
>> trigger the flicker events. If you can pull the files, you see these
>> events periodically throughout the recording period. Just keep us in the
>> loop and we are more than happy to help to get this resolved.
>>
>>
>>
>>
>>
>> Joe
>>
>>
>>
>> *From:* Charlie Joyce <cjoyce@hendrickspower.com>
>> *Sent:* Thursday, June 4, 2026 4:33 PM
>> *To:* Brandon Conard <brandon@solrivercapital.com>
>> *Cc:* Joe Parsons <jparsons@hendrickspower.com>; Greg Ternet <
>> gregternet@hendrickspower.com>
>> *Subject:* Re: Hendricks/SolRiver - C&B Graham PPA
>>
>>
>>
>> Brandon,
>>
>>
>>
>> Per our discussion today, please see the attached power quality
>> screenshots and files. I have included two snips per transformer, a Total
>> Power graph, and an IFL Flicker graph. Each is from the morning of 6/1, and
>> each is representative of the greater issue we have been experiencing. The
>> software (ProVision) required to open and analyze the files can be found on
>> the manufacturer's website, here
>> <https://powermonitors.com/product-category/software/>.
>>
>>
>>
>> The IFL flicker graph is less telling in terms of root cause, but I think
>> it provides a good perspective of the magnitude of this situation. For
>> reference, IFL is a metric used to measure instantaneous flicker levels. An
>> IFL reading of 1.0 represents the threshold of human annoyance. As the
>> attached snips will show, during these events we are recording IFL readings
>> nearly 40 times the threshold of annoyance.
>>
>>
>>
>> Within the Total Power graph, I would draw your attention to the
>> fluctuation shown on kVAR graph in rel…
2026-06-16 RE: Wallace Solar: Allowed to operate at full capacity Portfolio (2 msgs · projects@solrivercapital.com) 
Good morning. I'm recycling this old email in hopes I can find the best contact for the Wallace Solar site located 6475 Wallace Road NW, Salem, OR. More specifically, wondering if you have remote access to the SEL relay on site to aid with some diagnostics.

[cid:image001.png]
Bryan Focht - PE
Protection Testing Engineer
System Protection * C: 541-760-3018 * P: 503-612-6112
Portland General Electric

From: Alix Lefler <alix.lefler@pgn.com>
Sent: Monday, March 3, 2025 3:22 PM
To: John Brown <projects@solrivercapital.com>; Michael Jackson <M.Jackson@KonistoCo.com>; Skylar Cotter <skylar@solrivercapital.com>; Riley Sullivan <riley@solrivercapital.com>
Subject: Wallace Solar: Allowed to operate at full capacity

Good afternoon, team,

We had a successful MBTT test at Wallace Solar last week! The site's transfer trip is currently online, and the site may now operate at full capacity.

As mentioned in our meeting, PGE will take the site temporarily offline on Friday March 7th to test at another site. I will email the Developer and EPC with offline/online updates on Friday.

Thank you all again for your attention and support of this project.

Please reach out with any questions or concerns.

Thank you,

[cid:image002.png]
Alix Lefler
Pronouns: she/her
Staff Construction Project Manager | 503-781-7670
portlandgeneral.com | Follow us on social @PortlandGeneral
An Oregon kind of energy.

2026-06-16 Duke Energy Memorandum: IEEE 1547-2018 Compliance - Ride-Through Settings Implementation Portfolio (3 msgs · GTP@duke-energy.com) 
INTERNAL

All,

You are receiving this email because you are listed as a contact for a particular DER facility in the DER Dashboard.

The Generator Testing & Performance team would like to share an update regarding Duke Energy's initiative to improve voltage and frequency ride-through performance for DER sites during Transmission transients. Duke Energy has started implementing these improved settings on selected utility-owned Point of Interconnect (POI) reclosers, and we plan to expand these settings updates to all POI reclosers soon.

As we move forward and to ensure full compliance with IEEE 1547-2018, all DER customers will need to update voltage and frequency settings at their respective DER sites in alignment with the Duke Energy standard. Please take a moment to review the attached memo, as it outlines the summary of the initiative, associated documents, and required action steps.

Upon receiving this memo, customers can begin the process of updating settings at their respective DER sites. Our team will continue to keep everyone informed and will send out reminders as setting updates are implemented for each DER facility's recloser.

The expected deadline for the implementation of these settings is July 1st 2027.

Please reach out if you have any questions.

Thank you,

Grid Integration - Generator Testing & Performance

[image: FGNW: Investors]

---

---

let's discuss putting this on the task list for long term

BRANDON CONARD
C.E.O. - SolRiver Capital LLC
o: 720-593-1158 | www.solrivercapital.com

The Generator Testing & Performance team would like to share an update regarding Duke Energy's initiative to improve voltage and frequency ride-through performance for DER sites during Transmission transients. Duke Energy has started implementing these improved settings on selected utility-owned Point of Interconnect (POI) reclosers, and we plan to expand these settings updates to all POI reclosers soon.

As we move forward and to ensure full compliance with IEEE 1547-2018, all DER customers will need to update voltage and frequency settings at their respective DER sites in alignment with the Duke Energy standard. Please take a moment to review the attached memo, as it outlines the summary of the initiative, associated documents, and required action steps.

Upon receiving this memo, customers can begin the process of updating settings at their respective DER sites. Our team will continue to keep everyone informed and will send out reminders as setting updates are implemented for each DER facility's recloser.

The expected deadline for the implementation of these settings is July 1st 2027.

Please reach out …
2026-06-16 Certification Deadline Approaching - Canyonville 1 and 2 Portfolio (2 msgs · administrator@oregoncsp.org) 
Hi SolRiver,  Canyonville Solar 1 and 2's Certification deadline is approaching (6/26/2026). Please review the Expectations for Project Certification document to ensure the projects meet all the Certification requirements. If the projects need more time to meet the Certification requirements, please submit a Certification deadline extension request letter to the PA team. In this letter, please ensure you explain each of these points:  - What progress has the project made? - What Certification requirements does the project still need to meet?  - Why has the project not met those Certification requirements?  - Provide a new deadline that will give the project enough time (plus a cushion) to meet the Certification requirements. If the extended request is a year or longer, please explain why the project needs a year or more to meet the requirements.   Please let me know if you have any questions Thanks,  Merissa Larson Program Administrator Oregon Community Solar Program administrator@oregoncsp.org

---

---

Team - we need to make sure we address this. Let's discuss next week. In
the meantime, let's start drafting a response.

BRANDON CONARD
Managing Partner – SolRiver Capital LLC
o: 720-593-1158 | www.solrivercapital.com

---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Merissa Larson <administrator@oregoncsp.org>
Date: Tue, Jun 16, 2026, 4:44 PM
Subject: Certification Deadline Approaching - Canyonville 1 and 2
To: <skylar@solrivercapital.com>
Cc: <brandon@solrivercapital.com>, <riley@solrivercapital.com>

Hi SolRiver,

Canyonville Solar 1 and 2's Certification deadline is approaching
(6/26/2026). Please review the Expectations for Project Certification
<https://www.oregoncsp.org/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/Expectations-for-Project-Certification-v251216.pdf>
document to ensure the projects meet all the Certification requirements. If
the projects need more time to meet the Certification requirements, please
submit a Certification deadline extension request letter to the PA team. In
this letter, please ensure you explain each of these points:
- What progress has the project made?
- What Certification requirements does the project still need to meet?
- Why has the project not met those Certification requirements?
- Provide a new deadline that will give the project enough time (plus a
cushion) to meet the Certification requirements. If the extended request is
a year or longer, please explain why the project needs a year or more to
meet the requirements.

Please let me know if you have any questions

Thanks,
Merissa Larson
Program Administrator

Oregon Community Solar Program
administrator@oregoncsp.org

===============================================================
2026-06-16 52. Longleaf and Williams solar - questions for filing NOT Portfolio (3 msgs · steve@solrivercapital.com) 
Hey Chris,

We've been notified that as the owners of Longleaf Pine Solar, LLC and Williams Solar, LLC we are responsible for filing the NOT regarding the stormwater and erosion permit closeout at both sites. We have access to the NCDEQ portal. Can you please explain how to file the NOT online for both sites?

Thank you,
Steve

--
Steve Maloney
Director of Asset Management – SolRiver LLC
o: 720-923-1983 | www.solriver.com

---

Good morning. I have attached a document here that hopefully will help navigating through the closure process. If you have further questions, please let me know.

Chris Bailey
Environmental Specialist I
DEQ – DEMLR
336-776-9722
chris.bailey@deq.nc.gov

[Attachment: HOW TO (Access Modify Terminate Existing Permit)_7.25.25 (002).pdf]

From: Steve Maloney <steve@solrivercapital.com>
Sent: Tuesday, June 16, 2026 3:59 PM
To: Bailey, Christopher G <chris.bailey@deq.nc.gov>
Cc: Maintenance <om@solrivercapital.com>

Hey Chris,

We've been notified that as the owners of Longleaf Pine Solar, LLC and Williams Solar, LLC we are responsible for filing the NOT regarding the stormwater and erosion permit closeout at both sites. We have access to the NCDEQ portal. Can you please explain how to file the NOT online for both sites?

Thank you,
Steve

--
Steve Maloney
Director of Asset Management – SolRiver LLC
o: 720-923-1983 | www.solriver.com

(Email correspondence to and from this address may be subject to the North Carolina Public Records Law.)

---

Hi Chris,

Jumping in to support Steve on this.

The instructions state that we must pay all pending invoices before filing the NOT. We made the payment last week and received a receipt, but the portal still shows the status as "Payment Due".

Is there an issue with the payment, or should we proceed with filing the NOT?

Best regards,
Abhinav

2026-06-15 Re: SR/NRCO - Elk Inverters Offline/Not Communicating Portfolio (14 msgs · om@solrivercapital.com) 
Abhinav,

I spoke with Newman late last week and I thought he had communicated what
was found. I called the technician and he said that he did an inspection of
the arrays and found additional theft on one inverter, He found that the
thieves had switched the breakers off on the rest of the inverters and he
was able to return those to service on 6/8. The police report is attached.
I am guessing the thieves came back now that the inverters are back
offline. We can make another site visit if you would like?

Thanks,

Jayme Orrock

Vice President of Construction

*NARENCO │ *National Renewable Energy Corporation

435-590-0549 (M)

jayme.orrock@narenco.com

2026-06-15 Re: Elk Solar - Smart Inverter Pilot Participant - Need Volt-VAR Enabled Portfolio (2 msgs · steve@solrivercapital.com) 
Jayme,

Hope you had a good weekend.

Can you please provide an update on Duke's comment regarding the Vol Var settings at Elk? Are you able to see the message?

Thank you,
Steve

On Mon, Jun 8, 2026 at 9:58 AM Steve Maloney <steve@solrivercapital.com> wrote:

> NRCO team,
>
> Please see the message below from Duke regarding volt var settings at Elk.
>
> Thanks,
> Steve
>
> ---------- Forwarded message ---------
> From: Miller, Trent <Trent.Miller@duke-energy.com>
> Date: Mon, Jun 8, 2026 at 9:42 AM
> Subject: Elk Solar - Smart Inverter Pilot Participant - Need Volt-VAR Enabled
> To: steve@solrivercapital.com <steve@solrivercapital.com>, projects@solrivercapital.com <projects@solrivercapital.com>
> Cc: accounting@solrivercapital.com <accounting@solrivercapital.com>, PPA <PPA@duke-energy.com>
>
>
> INTERNAL
>
> Good Morning,
>
> We've been tracking the operation of the Elk Solar facility following the return to the normal circuit arrangement and the removal of the 2MW limit on the facility. The volt-VAR/volt-Watt functionality does not appear to be enabled and is causing over voltage conditions as a result of the under-absorption of reactive power. The site may still be operating with a constant power factor mode. To remedy these issues, please re-enable the volt-VAR/volt-Watt functions at this site. If there is an issue with enabling these functions, please let me know so that we can develop what the plan for this site should be.
>
> Thank you.
>
> *Trent Miller*
> *Smart Inverter Pilot Program Lead*
> Grid Integration - Distributed Energy Group
>
> --
> *Steve Maloney*
> Director of Asset Management – *SolRiver **LLC*
> *o:* 720-923-1983 | *www.solriver.com <http://www.solriver.com/>*

--
*Steve Maloney*
Director of Asset Management – *SolRiver **LLC*
*o:* 720-923-1983 | *www.solriver.com <http://www.solriver.com/>*

---

---

Steve,

We are trying to get Aderis' assistance with this.

Thanks,

Jayme Orrock

Vice President of Construction

*NARENCO | *National Renewable Energy Corporation

435-590-0549 (M)

2026-06-15 Re: SR/AlsoEnergy - C&B Graham DAS project Portfolio (3 msgs · om@solrivercapital.com) 
Steve,

I've supplied a modem/cell plan as part of the BOM here attached. There should be room in the main DAS (WT200) to install that modem, the antenna should be installed exterior to any buildings.

Our cell plan contains a VPN, we will just need to know what outbound ports Solectria will need us to open up to allow for outbound access to their portal. Does Solectria have a specific gateway onsite as well? I'm not sure that there will be room for that in our DAS enclosure, just a heads up.

I've attached a quote for 12 hours of project management and remote commissioning services to assist with the support and re-commissioning of the DAS units. Once you submit a PO or return this signed quote, I'll introduce you to the project manager that is assigned.

Let me know if you have any questions!

Best Regards,

Jack Stringer

Account Manager – Southwest Region

720-466-8595 • Portland, OR
Learn more at stem.com ><https://www.stem.com/>
[cid:14637d5c-eca7-45e0-849b-7109e9e698b8]

Hey Jack, Thanks for jumping on this. I am not sure if we need recloser monitoring, but we'll review it. I have a few additional updates below to share with you and to pass along. Does the current Also Energy gear have a VPN and router? If not,
Warning: Unusual link
This message contains an unusual link, which may lead to a malicious site. Confirm the message is safe before clicking any links.
Hey Jack,

Thanks for jumping on this. I am not sure if we need recloser monitoring, but we'll review it. I have a few additional updates below to share with you and to pass along.

Does the current Also Energy gear have a VPN and router? If not, can we order this from you or is this not needed for this site?

For context, we have Solectria xgi1500 inverters installed here and these Inverters can be monitored and controlled through the Yaskawa Solectria portal. I think this portal requires a VPN connection for access. Additionally, I was told the site's VPN and router for this connection is located in the Solview/Solential DAS box - the boxes we need removed. I'm told the AlsoEnergy boxes do not have a VPN router.

Additionally, if it's not clear in the plans, I believe the Solview/Solential DAS boxes contain the following cables and con…

2026-06-15 Re: Green Solar CUP citation Portfolio (1 msg · steve@solrivercapital.com) 
Ralph,

Thank you for the call today. We appreciate your support as we work toward
a solution with the county.

To recap our conversation: you are not aware of any accessible water lines
on site, hence why it is designated non-irrigated, but you offered access
to your supply (approximately 1,000 ft away) as one option. You also
suggested we reach out to your neighbor adjacent to the site to potentially
use his water. I have his contact info and will follow up with him if need
be.

You also plan to attend Thursday's hearing to hear the county's position
directly. Regarding screening, you suggested installing fence screening
which we have also been discussing internally and hope the county will
consider favorably.

I will keep you posted as things develop. Thanks again.

Best,
Steve

On Mon, Jun 15, 2026 at 10:06 AM Ralph Lewis <rlewis54982@gmail.com> wrote:

>
>
>
> Sent from my iPhone

--
*Steve Maloney*
Director of Asset Management – *SolRiver **LLC*
*o:* 720-923-1983 | *www.solriver.com <http://www.solriver.com/>*

2026-06-15 SC Property Tax return for DuRant Farm Solar installation in Timmonsville SC Portfolio (3 msgs · donnawinchester125@gmail.com) 
All, I have received the attached notice from the South Carolina Department
of Revenue. Per the underlined section, this form requires information that
I do not have but that I'm sure you do. Please communicate with me on this
matter so that we can file before any penalty or assessment is levied.
 Thank you.

Donna DuRant Winchester

---

---

All, I have talked to the South Carolina Department of revenue about the
property tax return. We are BOTH required to file the form. I must file as
the landowner re the value of the property. SolRiver must file with regards
to the upgrades and installations on the property. Please let me know when
filed.

Thanks!
Donna

2026-06-15 36. SR/NRCO - Whitetail Site Offline Portfolio (8 msgs · abhinav@solrivercapital.com) 
Hi Jayme,

Whitetail appears to have gone offline around 6:45 PM on 6/14, and AlsoEnergy currently shows an open relay alert.

Could you please investigate and confirm the cause of the outage?

Best regards,
Abhinav

[image attachments]

--
Abhinav Korni
Asst. Project Manager – SolRiver Capital LLC
o: (720) 642-6779 | www.solrivercapital.com

---

---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: OM Operations <omops@narenco.com>
Date: Tue, Jun 16, 2026 at 9:03 AM
Subject: Re: SR/NRCO - Whitetail Site Offline
To: Abhinav Korni <abhinav@solrivercapital.com>
Cc: Jayme Orrock <jayme.orrock@narenco.com>

Abhinav,

The site tripped offline due to a dip in utility voltage, and the NCC attempted to close it in remotely. While the remote close command was successfully transmitted to the RVC, the breaker did not re-close, which prompted us to dispatch a technician to the site.

Our technician investigated the site outage and identified a likely issue with the AC wires between the RVC and the AC cabinet. The B and C phases did not completely fail but they did not perform well in insulation testing, suggesting the wiring may be deteriorating.

The recommended corrective action is to replace the affected conductors. The technician also noted that the dry type transformer within the RVC may be contributing to the breaker trips and will be evaluated further during repairs.

We will provide an update once a repair plan and schedule have been finalized.

Please let us know if you have any questions.

Thank you!
Jacob Budd
(NARENCO Operations Center)

> [Quoted: Abhinav's original 6/15 outage email]

--
Abhinav Korni
Asst. Project Manager – SolRiver Capital LLC
o: (720) 642-6779 | www.solrivercapital.com

---

Jayme -
Last year, when your tech thought the RVC was the reason Whitetail was down, it turned out to be the Main AC breaker. Did your team check the breaker?
How long is the warranty on that part?
What does it cost to buy a new main AC breaker?
How would the dry type transformer in the RVC contribute to breaker trips? If something is wrong with it, what is wrong? What causes that to go wrong?

BRANDON CONARD
C.E.O. – SolRiver Capital LLC
o: 720-593-1158 | www.solrivercapital.com

> [Quoted: Jacob Budd's 6/16 diagnosis and Abhinav's original email]

---

Brandon,

A failing dry type transformer can cause voltage issues or draw excess current which can lead to nuisance trips or keep the breaker from closing properly. That being said, the technician thinks its most likely to be the wiring between the RVC and AC cabinet, so that's currently the most likely cause of the problem. But both will be investigated.
Jacob Budd

> [Quoted: prior chain]

---

Thanks..what about the main breaker? Did you investigate that?

BRANDON CONARD
Managing Partner – SolRiver Capital LLC
o: 720-593-1158 | www.solrivercapital.com

> [Quoted: prior chain]

---

Brandon,

The 150A AC Breaker for the RVC in the switchboard cabinet, not the main breaker. This breaker was replaced in July and…

department.

Thanks! - Wendy

---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Lisa Hawley <Lisa.Hawley@douglascountyor.gov>
Date: Tue, Jun 2, 2026 at 10:24 AM
Subject: RE: SR/Douglas County - Canyonville Solar
To: Matt La Rocca <larocca@solrivercapital.com>
Cc: Abhinav Korni <abhinav@solrivercapital.com>, John Brown <
projects@solrivercapital.com>, Steve Maloney <steve@solrivercapital.com>,
Wendy Garduno <wendy@solrivercapital.com>, Jeff Lehrbach <
Jeff.Lehrbach@douglascountyor.gov>

Good morning, Matt,

Thank you for your email. I apologize for the delayed response.

I'm going to start preparing a draft MOU agreement to send to our County
Counsel. Can you confirm and email me the following information?

Company Name ? (is it Canyonville Solar LLC?)

ATTN: ? (staff person for contact)

Address?

City, State, Zip Code ?

Email Address: ? (email address for contact)

Name of person authorized to sign on behalf of Canyonville Solar LLC?

Thank you,

Lisa

* LISA HAWLEY - SENIOR PLANNER*

OR 97470

On Wed, Apr 15, 2026 at 10:16 AM Lisa Hawley <
Lisa.Hawley@douglascountyor.gov> wrote:

Hi Matt,

It's been awhile, but I want to give you an update on Cond #4. We've heard
back from our County Counsel, and she thinks the type of Memorandum of
Understanding (MOU) that Linn County used for a recent solar facility near
Albany would work for Douglas County. It appears it will be easier to
enforce over the lifespan of the facility.

I've converted the example of the Linn County agreement into a word
document and will forward it to our County Counsel to draft an agreement.
Once drafted, this agreement will need to be reviewed by the affected
parties (i.e., Douglas County and Canyonville Solar).

Attached is a redacted copy of the Linn County MOU to get you an…
